restaurants.name: DON JULIO PARRILLA
restaurants.notes: Parrilla Don Julio, located in the heart of Buenos Aires’ ever-vibrant Palermo district, continues to stand as one of the most iconic institutions on the global culinary map. As we step into 2025, its legacy only deepens - a restaurant where the essence of Argentine cuisine and heartfelt hospitality is celebrated with unmatched dedication. At the helm is Pablo Rivero - owner, sommelier and visionary - who has long been recognised as a pioneering force in the world of meat and wine. Under his leadership, Don Julio has transformed the classic parrilla experience into something extraordinary, where tradition meets refinement without ever losing its soul. The restaurant’s acclaim rests not only on its masterfully grilled meats but also on its award-winning wine programme, which Pablo personally curates to complement the depth and character of Argentine beef. Every pairing reflects his expert understanding of flavour, terroir and harmony - elevating each meal into a layered, sensorial experience that continues to draw meat lovers and gastronomes from across the globe. Working in tandem with Pablo is Guido Tassi, known as the Meat Master, whose unparalleled knowledge of butchery and dry-aging continues to define the Don Julio standard. His careful craftsmanship is evident in every cut that reaches the plate - a testament to time, patience and absolute respect for the product. The setting remains as inviting as ever - rustic yet elegant, with shelves of wine bottles signed by guests from around the world, each one a story in itself. The service is polished and sincere, with a team that radiates the same passion and hospitality Pablo brings to every aspect of the restaurant. In 2025, Don Julio’s commitment to quality and sustainability remains unwavering. Meat is sourced from trusted local producers and aged to perfection in the in-house butchery next door, with a strong emphasis on ethical farming, animal welfare and natural grazing - principles that are not only admirable but deeply flavourful. Parrilla Don Julio is more than a restaurant - it is the living embodiment of Pablo Rivero’s lifelong dedication to the craft of meat and wine. A beacon of Argentine excellence, it remains - once again - our No. 1 of the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants, offering a timeless yet ever-evolving celebration of fire, heritage and hospitality.

restaurants.name: MARGARET
restaurants.notes: Margaret - Neil Perry’s Grand Celebration of Land and Sea in Sydney In 2025, Margaret continues to shine as one of Australia’s most important culinary destinations- a restaurant that effortlessly balances elegant sophistication with heartfelt hospitality. Located in the stylish suburb of Double Bay, this flagship venture by legendary Australian chef Neil Perry has firmly established itself as a showcase of the very best Australia has to offer - both from the land and the sea. At Margaret, beef and seafood don’t compete - they coexist as centrepieces of a thoughtful, ingredient-driven menu. Perry’s lifelong commitment to provenance and sustainability is on full display here, and every dish carries the confidence of a chef who has spent decades at the pinnacle of Australian gastronomy. On the meat side, the offering is nothing short of world-class. A selection of the finest breeds and cuts from Australia’s top producers like Blackmore Wagyu - Australia’s benchmark in full blood Wagyu excellence - and also CopperTree Farms with its beef from retired Holstein-Friesian dairy cows are cooked over a wood-fired grill with stunning precision. The steaks are charred and caramelised outside, tender and expressive inside, with seasoning that respects the integrity of each cut. Margaret is not only one of the best steak destinations in the world - it is also one of the most refined. Yet what makes this restaurant truly unique is Perry’s equal devotion to Australian seafood. Line-caught coral trout, Glacier 51 toothfish, Mooloolaba king prawns and Fraser Isle spanner crab arrive at the table in preparations that are clean, bright, and elegant, often kissed by fire or elevated by subtle Asian influences - a nod to Perry’s deep connection with pan-Pacific flavour profiles. The wine list is intelligent and expansive, with a strong emphasis on Australian producers and sustainable winemaking. The front-of-house team, led by seasoned professionals, moves with polished ease - friendly, informed and always a few steps ahead. The atmosphere is both contemporary and timeless. Warm, natural materials, an open kitchen and generous lighting give the dining room an energy that feels as local as it does global. In 2025, Margaret stands proudly among the world’s best. It is a restaurant that celebrates Australia’s culinary identity through its finest produce - from pasture to coastline - and presents it with clarity, skill and soul. Under Neil Perry’s visionary leadership, it is not only a place to eat - it’s a place to understand what makes Australian dining truly exceptional. Margaret - the proud No. 2 of our list of the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants.

restaurants.name: LAIA ERRETEGIA
restaurants.notes: Restaurant Laia run by the Ayala family, located in the picturesque town of Hondarribia, Spain, offers a truly unique dining experience that seamlessly blends traditional Basque culinary techniques with innovative, modern twists. Set against a backdrop of historic charm, the restaurant's ambiance is warm and inviting, perfect for both casual diners and gourmet enthusiasts. The menu at Laia is a testament to the rich culinary heritage of the Basque region, authentic grilled flavours, focusing on locally sourced ingredients and seasonal offerings with a strong emphasis on quality meats. Highlights include their 60 days dry-aged Côte de Boeuf from local breeds, prepared with unrivalled precision that brings out all characteristics of this amazing beef quality. The creativity of the kitchen shines through in dishes like the „riz avec palourdes“ and the grilled scallops. What sets Laia apart is not just its food but also its impeccable service helmed by Arantza the sister of grill master and head chef Jon. The staff is knowledgeable and eager to share their passion for Basque cuisine, providing recommendations and explaining the origins and preparation of each dish. This enhances the overall dining experience, making each meal both educational and enjoyable. Additionally, the restaurant boasts a carefully curated selection of wines that complement their dishes beautifully, featuring both local and international vintages. The pairing suggestions provided by Arantza can elevate the meal to an extraordinary culinary journey. Restaurant Laia is more than just a place to eat; it is a destination where food lovers can indulge in a rich tapestry of flavours and textures. It's a must-visit for anyone looking to explore the best of what Basque cuisine has to offer.

restaurants.name: I DUE CIPPI
restaurants.notes: I Due Cippi - Tuscan Tradition with a Flame-Cooked Heart Nestled in the heart of Saturnia, amidst rolling Tuscan hills and centuries-old thermal springs, I Due Cippi stands as a beacon of culinary excellence in southern Tuscany. For over three decades, this family-run restaurant has welcomed travellers and locals alike with its unmistakable charm, open-flame cooking and steadfast commitment to quality. At the centre of the experience is the restaurant’s wood-fired grill, visible from the dining room, where a curated selection of meats is cooked to perfection. Each cut is treated with reverence, grilled over glowing embers that lend a smoky complexity to the already rich, marbled beef. The meat is mainly sourced from selected regional producers, aged in-house and served simply - allowing the ingredients to shine in their most honest form. Beyond the grill, I Due Cippi excels in Tuscan classics elevated with just the right amount of finesse. Starters like local pecorino drizzled with honey, or handmade pici with wild boar ragù, speak to the restaurant’s deep roots in the land. Everything on the menu feels connected to the rhythm of the Maremma - seasonal, rustic and deeply satisfying. Service at I Due Cippi reflects the genuine hospitality of the region. Alessandro Aniello, co-owner Maître D’ and head of wine, plays a central role in guiding guests through the extensive and thoughtfully composed wine list, featuring a rich selection of Tuscan and Italian vintages. His pairing suggestions elevate the meal without pretension - always in the service of pleasure and balance. The atmosphere is warm and lively, a blend of stone, firelight and laughter, where one feels more like a welcome guest in someone’s home than a customer in a restaurant. Whether seated in the cosy interior or the charming outdoor space under the Saturnian sky, guests are enveloped in a sense of timeless Italian hospitality. In 2025, I Due Cippi remains a quintessential Tuscan dining experience helmed by chef-owner Lorenzo Aniello - a place where fire, excellence, passion and family intertwine. For lovers of fine meat, great wine and honest cooking, it’s nothing short of essential and makes it the best steak restaurant in Italy and our current No. 4 of the list.

restaurants.name: BURNT ENDS
restaurants.notes: Singapore’s Open-Flame Icon Reaches Global Heights In the ever-evolving world of wood-fired cuisine, few restaurants have achieved the level of cult status and global recognition that Burnt Ends now enjoys. Tucked into Singapore’s vibrant Dempsey Hill, this modern Australian barbecue restaurant, led by the visionary Dave Pynt, has quickly become one of the most talked-about steak destinations worldwide. In 2025, Burnt Ends entered the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants ranking as the highest new entry, a testament to its uncompromising dedication to flavour, fire and finesse. This is not your traditional steakhouse—it’s a place where primal technique meets polished execution, where every dish tells a story of craftsmanship and bold creativity. At the heart of the experience is the custom-built four-tonne dual cavity wood-fired oven, flanked by elevation grills that allow for precision control. The results speak for themselves: an ever-changing menu where steaks like Australian Blackmore Wagyu or Margra lamb are seared to perfection, with a unique kiss of smoke and a luscious char that lingers on the palate. But it’s not just the steak that turns heads. Pynt and his team have a deep respect for the full beast and seasonal produce, creating dishes that are as surprising as they are satisfying—from smoked quail eggs and king crab with garlic brown butter, to beef marmalade with house-baked brioche. Every plate is built on layers of texture, aroma and elemental flavour. The dining space is intimate and buzzy, built around an open kitchen that blurs the lines between chef and guest. You don’t just eat at Burnt Ends—you experience the theatre of fire. The service is sharp yet relaxed and the wine list is as forward-thinking as the food, with a focus on natural and minimal-intervention producers from around the world. Under the leadership of Chef-Owner Dave Pynt, Burnt Ends has redefined what a steak restaurant can be—not only in Singapore but on the global stage. Its inclusion in the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants is not a surprise; it’s a well-deserved recognition of one of the most exciting meat-focused kitchens anywhere in the world. For those seeking fire-led cuisine with attitude and precision, Burnt Ends is nothing short of a revelation.

restaurants.name: BODEGA EL CAPRICHO
restaurants.notes: Bodega El Capricho - The Sacred Temple of Ox in 2025 Deep in the heart of Castilla y León, in the quiet village of Jiménez de Jamuz, lies a place that has become near-mythical among meat aficionados worldwide. In 2025, Bodega El Capricho, under the continued guardianship of José Gordon, remains the benchmark for what beef - when raised, aged and prepared with reverence - can truly be. This is more than a restaurant. It’s a philosophy, a personal vision brought to life by Gordon, whose deep understanding of oxen and commitment to sustainable, closed-cycle farming has set global standards. At El Capricho, “farm to fork” is not a slogan - it’s a lived ethic. Every animal is bred, raised and aged with patience and care, often for several years, before it reaches the grill. The meat itself - particularly the venerable ox steaks - is extraordinary. Aged with surgical precision in natural cellars carved into the earth, each cut bursts with depth, minerality and tenderness that borders on the transcendent. The ox tongue, served melting and the house-cured cecina, as well as the ox morcilla, are quiet revelations. These aren’t just dishes - they’re expressions of a culture and craft few others dare to pursue with such intensity. The culinary execution is led by Head Chef Diego Zárate, who works in seamless collaboration with Gordon to ensure the product’s essence is never overshadowed by technique. Everything on the plate honours the beast - from fire-grilled ribs with charred edges to marrow-rich broths infused with ancestral soul. The setting is rustic, authentic and steeped in history - an atmospheric mix of wine barrels, cast iron and local stone. Diners can watch their cuts kissed by flame on the open grill, with smoke rising into the vaulted ceilings and the scent of ember and fat filling the air. It’s the kind of place where time slows and every detail feels intentional. The extensive wine list, curated to mirror the intensity of the meat, rounds off the experience. It’s not uncommon for José himself to emerge from the cellar or grill to explain a pairing or tell the story of a particular ox - a reminder that this is not a restaurant chain or concept, but a living, breathing legacy. In 2025, Bodega El Capricho continues to stand as one of the world’s most singular meat temples - an essential pilgrimage for anyone serious about beef. It is not just a place to eat, but to feel: the land, the history, the fire and above all, the profound respect for the animal.

restaurants.name: CASA JULIAN DE TOLOSA
restaurants.notes: Casa Julián de Tolosa - The Original Temple of Basque Beef In the heart of the quiet Basque town of Tolosa, nestled between verdant hills and the Oria river, lies a culinary institution that has defined fire-grilled meat for generations: Casa Julián de Tolosa. Founded in 1951, this modest, wood-panelled restaurant has grown into a global beacon for steak purists, with a reputation that stretches far beyond Spain’s borders. In 2025, Casa Julián’s enduring legacy has once again been recognised on the world stage, gaining its position as No. 7 on the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants list - a remarkable accolade that reflects both its uncompromising philosophy and its timeless approach to beef. At its core is the legendary Txuletón: thick, bone-in ribeye steaks from mature Galician or local Basque cows, dry-aged and grilled exclusively over oak coals. The meat, deeply marbled and rich in character, is handled with near-spiritual reverence. No sauces, no distractions - just coarse salt, the kiss of flame and decades of instinct honed at the grill. Today, the Gorrotxategi family continues to helm the restaurant with quiet pride. Xabier Gorrotxategi, son of the late Matías, has not only upheld his father’s purist philosophy, but elevated it - refining ageing processes, meat sourcing and technique, while keeping every detail true to Casa Julián’s humble, fire-forward soul. The restaurant itself is delightfully unadorned: white-tiled walls, rustic furnishings and the unmistakable warmth of the open grill, always within sight of diners in the main dining room. There’s no music - just the crackle of fire and the rhythm of confident hands turning meat over hot embers. This is where legends are made, not performed. The supporting cast is equally essential. The piquillo peppers, peeled and roasted by hand and the simple tomato salad offer perfect balance - earthy, sweet and clean. The wine list is compact but purposeful, showcasing structured Spanish reds that stand up to the intensity of the grill. Dining at Casa Julián de Tolosa is not just a meal - it is a moment in culinary history, still alive and still burning. For those seeking authenticity, tradition and the highest expression of Basque beef, it remains not only one of the best steak restaurants in Spain, but one of the greatest meat restaurants in the world.

restaurants.name: LANA
restaurants.notes: Lana - Madrid’s Modern Shrine to Fire, Elegance and Argentine Passion In the vibrant heart of Madrid’s Chamberí district, Lana has swiftly ascended from insider tip to international heavyweight. Under the stewardship of Argentine brothers Martín and Joaquín Narvaiz, the restaurant has, in 2025, not only refined its offering but transformed itself into one of the most visually striking and technically precise steak restaurants in Europe. Its meteoric rise is no coincidence. Recognised once again in the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants ranking, Lana has made one of the most impressive leaps year-over-year, now firmly established among the top global destinations for serious meat lovers. The culinary focus remains rooted in the Narvaiz brothers’ Argentine heritage, but with unmistakable Spanish precision and European polish. Premium cuts are grilled over Argentine quebracho wood, imparting a deep, smoky complexity. Yet Lana is far more than a steak restaurant. The kitchen has evolved beyond its already strong foundations, with 2025 showcasing remarkable depth across the menu. Highlights now include smoked beef tartare with bone marrow, empanadas reinvented with gourmet finesse and charred seasonal vegetables that reflect the same care and respect as the proteins. The maturation of the concept is evident in every detail. The dining room itself is one of the most beautiful settings in the entire ranking—minimalist yet warm, where fire, iron and natural wood set the tone. A open kitchen draws the eye, offering guests a view of the flames and the team’s choreographed intensity. It is elegant, inviting and unmistakably modern. The impressive wine programme, too, has been elevated. Spanish and South American producers dominate the list, with bold Malbecs, subtle Riojas and a growing selection of biodynamic and limited-production bottles offering diners both structure and discovery. But what truly sets Lana apart in 2025 is its balance—between rustic fire and urban sophistication, between tradition and evolution. Service is attentive yet relaxed and the Narvaiz brothers remain a visible presence in the room, their passion for hospitality woven into every dish, every detail, every ember. For those seeking one of the most complete steak restaurant experiences in Europe, Lana is no longer a rising star—it is a burning flame, steady and bright. A restaurant that has earned its place not just through meat, but through soul, precision and extraordinary growth.

restaurants.name: AG
restaurants.notes: AG - Stockholm’s Inner Sanctum of Scandinavian Meat Mastery Hidden away in a former silver factory on Kungsholmen, AG is Stockholm’s worst-kept secret—and proudly so. With its masculine interiors, stainless steel details and moody lighting, this is not merely a restaurant. It is Sweden’s ultimate homage to meat, a serious, confident steakhouse that has set the bar for Nordic carnivores for over a decade. In 2025, AG doesn’t just maintain its place among the world’s elite - it asserts it more clearly than ever, thanks in no small part to the ongoing excellence of Martin Kjäll, the restaurant’s longtime Meat Master, who this year was recognised with the coveted Meat Master of the Year award by us. His influence is unmistakable in every cut that enters the kitchen, from sourcing and aging to precise trimming and final execution. The AG beef programme is among the most ambitious in the world. Swedish dairy cow beef, aged for up to 90 days in AG’s signature dry-ageing room, is a house speciality - its flavour deep, nutty and entirely unique. Alongside it, you’ll find Galician old cow, Australian Wagyu and meticulously selected Japanese A5, all cooked over high-heat charcoal grills for maximum flavour and contrast. Every steak is a study in provenance, marbling and maturity. AG’s ethos is grounded in transparency and craft. You see the meat. You choose your cut. You taste the difference. Even the house burger, served with bone marrow mayonnaise and pickled onions, is a masterclass in balance and indulgence. The space itself exudes a moody urban elegance - think butcher hooks, black leather and open flames. The dry-ageing room is on display like a museum exhibit and the wine cellar offers everything from muscular Rioja to crisp northern whites - perfect for navigating a menu that ranges from bold to subtle. Service is slick, casual and informed - never pretentious, always efficient. The sommelier team works with precision, guiding guests through both classic pairings and offbeat gems. In 2025, AG is not simply Sweden’s best steak restaurant - it is one of Europe’s most complete meat destinations and ranked within the Top 10 of our list. With Johan Jureskog at the helm, curating one of the continent’s most refined beef selections, the restaurant is a testament to how far passion and precision can go when they are given the room to burn.

restaurants.name: COTE
restaurants.notes: COTE - New York In the heart of Manhattan’s Flatiron District, COTE has redefined what a modern steakhouse can be. Combining the elegance and rigour of a classic American meat temple with the communal fire and finesse of Korean barbecue, this gastronomic powerhouse delivers a dining experience that’s as theatrical as it is refined. Since its opening, COTE has drawn global attention - not only for its sleek interior and vibrant atmosphere, but for its deep commitment to exceptional beef. In 2025, it stands as one of New York’s most dynamic steak destinations, with a steady presence in the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants and a cult following that spans continents. At the heart of the restaurant’s concept is the in-table smokeless grill, a nod to Korean tradition, but elevated by chef-level precision. Guests are invited to experience curated cuts of USDA Prime, American Wagyu and A5 Japanese Miyazaki, all dry-aged in-house in a state-of-the-art ageing room. Each piece is grilled tableside by trained staff, ensuring consistent perfection in doneness, crust and temperature. The signature Butcher’s Feast offers a guided journey through COTE’s finest offerings - starting with banchan, house-made kimchi and pickled seasonal vegetables, before moving through an exquisitely balanced progression of beef. It’s communal, interactive and deeply satisfying - bringing the warmth of Korean hospitality into sharp focus. But COTE is more than show. The attention to detail across the board is uncompromising. From the marbling of a single cut to the expertly curated wine list and cocktail programme, everything is executed with the precision of fine dining. The restaurant’s sleek, low-lit interior hums with energy - equal parts high-design lounge and smoky, bustling Korean grill house. Behind the vision is Simon Kim, the Korean-American restaurateur who has built COTE into a brand of global influence, with offshoots in Miami, Singapore and beyond. His ability to fuse cultures without compromise has helped position COTE as not just a great steak restaurant, but a genre-defining dining experience and our best steak restaurant in North America. In 2025, COTE remains one of New York’s most compelling meat-driven destinations. It’s a place where technique meets fire, where East meets West and where every table becomes its own private grill-side celebration.

restaurants.name: IBAI
restaurants.notes: London’s Basque Steak Restaurant of Fire and Flair Tucked away in the heart of Farringdon, Ibai brings the soul of the Basque Country to London’s dining scene. Founded by Nemanja Borjanović and William Sheard of Txuleta, alongside Head Chef Richard Foster—formerly of Chiltern Firehouse—Ibai is a celebration of exceptional beef, open-fire cooking and bold, rustic elegance. Chef Richard Foster, renowned for his tenure at Chiltern Firehouse, leads the kitchen with a focus on showcasing the depth and character of Basque cuisine. His expertise ensures that each dish, from the simplest starter to the most complex main, is executed with precision and passion. At the heart of Ibai’s menu lies the Galician Blond beef, sourced from retired dairy cows aged between 8 to 12 years. These cuts, including T-bone, sirloin and rib, are known for their deep, mature flavours and are grilled to perfection over a custom-built charcoal grill. Other standout dishes include: • Croque Ibai: A decadent toastie filled with boudin noir, carabinero prawns and Tomme de Brebis cheese, finished with a drizzle of honey. • King Crab Rice: A luxurious dish featuring succulent king crab atop rich, bisque-infused rice. • Beef Tartare: Brightened with Espelette pepper, offering a vibrant twist on a classic. Ibai employs a multi-tiered charcoal grill, allowing for precise control over cooking temperatures. This method imparts a distinctive smoky flavour to the meats, enhancing their natural qualities without overpowering them. The result is a perfect sear and a juicy, tender interior that showcases the beef’s inherent richness. Set in a converted warehouse, Ibai’s interior combines industrial chic with warm, rustic elements. Exposed steel beams, polished concrete floors and rich oak furnishings create an inviting atmosphere. The open kitchen allows diners to witness the culinary artistry firsthand, adding an element of theatre to the dining experience. Ibai our second highest new entry of the list stands as a testament to the enduring allure of Basque cuisine, offering a dining experience that is both authentic and innovative. With its commitment to quality ingredients, expert preparation and inviting ambience, Ibai has firmly established itself as a must-visit destination for steak enthusiasts and lovers of fine Basque inspired dining alike.

restaurants.name: ROCKPOOL BAR & GRILL
restaurants.notes: Rockpool Bar & Grill Sydney - Heritage, Flame and Precision Housed in the grand art deco surroundings of the historic City Mutual Building, Rockpool Bar & Grill in Sydney has long been a cornerstone of Australia’s fine dining scene. In 2025, under the talented leadership of Executive Chef Santiago Aristizábal, the restaurant continues to redefine what a modern steak restaurant can be - elegant, ingredient-driven and quietly daring. With its cathedral-high ceilings, marble columns and leather-clad banquettes, Rockpool offers one of the most iconic dining rooms in the Southern Hemisphere. But it is not the setting alone that earns its place among the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants - it’s what emerges from the kitchen and more specifically, the wood-fired grill. Chef Aristizábal brings a rare combination of technical precision and culinary soul. Trained across some of the most demanding kitchens in Australia, his stewardship has ushered in a renewed focus on provenance, dry-aging and cooking methods that honour the inherent character of each cut. The result is a menu that celebrates both Australian Wagyu and grass-fed beef, with each steak grilled over ironbark charcoal to achieve a perfect, caramelised crust and a deep, complex interior. But Rockpool is no one-trick pony. The kitchen shines across the board—from signature raw seafood starters like Hiramasa kingfish with citrus and herbs, to handmade pasta and vegetable side dishes that showcase peak-season produce with understated finesse. Yet it’s always the steak - be it a Cape Grim rib-eye or a David Blackmore Wagyu striploin—that draws the most reverent silence when it lands at the table. The wine list is one of the most lauded in the country, with thousands of bottles covering the world’s major regions and a strong emphasis on benchmark Australian estates. The in-house sommeliers are more guides than gatekeepers, making expert pairings accessible to all levels of wine knowledge. What elevates Rockpool Bar & Grill beyond the expected is its seamless blend of grandeur and groundedness. Service is crisp but never stiff; the atmosphere vibrant yet intimate.

restaurants.name: ASADOR BASTIAN
restaurants.notes: Asador Bastian - Basque Soul and Chicago Fire in Perfect Harmony In a historic brownstone nestled just off Chicago’s Gold Coast, Asador Bastian has redefined the Midwestern steak restaurant by looking not westward - but across the Atlantic, toward the Basque Country. In 2025, this elegant asador stands as the second-highest ranked steak restaurant in North America - a well-earned accolade for a concept rooted in purity, provenance and flame. Unlike the grand, clubby Chicago steakhouses of old, Asador Bastian brings a more intimate, curated approach to fire-led dining. The centrepiece is the txuletón - thick, bone-in ribeye from old dairy cows, aged with care and grilled over live embers in the traditional Basque style. The result is bold, deeply flavoured beef with complex marbling and an earthy depth that speaks of both animal and fire. Led by chef-owner Doug Psaltis, chef- partner Christian Eckmann and hospitality professionals who understand the delicate balance between rustic tradition and modern elegance, Bastian marries warmth and sophistication effortlessly. The beef, much of it imported from Galicia or specially raised in the US to mimic the qualities of Spanish old cow, is dry-aged in-house and grilled in the open kitchen - always in sight, always honest. The space is beautiful yet grounded: polished wood, soft lighting, exposed brick and subtle ironwork nod to the Basque taverns that inspired the concept. It’s intimate, quiet in all the right ways and gives centre stage to the fire, the product and the people who serve it. The wine programme is exceptional - structured around Spanish reds, French classics and elegant new-world pairings, all chosen to complement the richness of the beef without overpowering its nuance. Service is sharp but unforced, led by a team that understands when to explain and when to simply let the food speak. In a city already famed for its beef, Asador Bastian has created something rare - a steak restaurant with emotional clarity and cultural depth. In 2025, it stands not just as one of the finest restaurants in Chicago, but as one of the most important steak destinations in the world.

restaurants.name: THE GRILL AT THE INTERNATIONAL
restaurants.notes: Urban Sophistication and Fire-Driven Excellence in Sydney The Grill at The International is a striking new chapter in the city’s fine dining scene - an experience that combines elegance, fire and flavour under the direction of acclaimed Head Chef Joel Bickford. Known for his finesse, produce-driven philosophy and deep respect for Australian terroir, Bickford has crafted a venue that is both refined and elemental. The Grill at The International exudes a quiet grandeur. From its soaring ceilings and curated art to soft leather banquettes and sweeping harbour views, every detail reflects a mood of understated exclusivity. Yet despite the glamour, there is a groundedness at the heart of the experience - one forged by fire and focused on the plate. The centrepiece of the menu is a premium meat programme that places The International firmly on the map for steak connoisseurs. Bickford sources Australia’s finest Wagyu and pasture-raised beef, often dry-aged on site and grills them over wood and charcoal to coax out deep, smoky complexity. Cuts such as the Rangers Valley Black Market ribeye and a rotating selection of heritage breeds showcase the kitchen’s precise, fire-driven technique. Complementary dishes carry the same balance of luxury and restraint - Wagyu Tongue skewer with flat bread, heirloom vegetables with buffalo milk, or raw scallops with cultured cream. Each plate reflects Bickford’s mastery of flavour layering and textural contrast, always anchored by the quality of the ingredients. The wine cellar is excellent, offering a tightly curated collection of Australian icons, European benchmarks and emerging natural labels helmed by Thomas Groeneveld. The team is sharp and intuitive, creating pairings that highlight both the fire in the kitchen and the elegance on the table. Service is sleek, discreet and immaculately orchestrated - delivered by a team that understands the rhythm of fine dining without ever losing a sense of warmth. The Grill at The International under Joel Bickford is not just a restaurant - it is a statement of intent: that Australian luxury dining can be bold, produce-driven and centred on fire. For those seeking a destination that celebrates both precision and place, The International is a must.

restaurants.name: BORN & BRED
restaurants.notes: Born & Bred - Seoul’s Pinnacle of Hanwoo Excellence Situated near Seoul’s Majang Meat Market, Born & Bred stands as a testament to Korea’s rich beef heritage. Founded by Jeong Sang-won, a third-generation butcher, the restaurant offers a multi-tiered dining experience that celebrates the nuances of premium Hanwoo beef. Culinary Leadership Jeong Sang-won’s deep-rooted connection to the Majang Meat Market, where his father still operates a butcher shop, informs his meticulous approach to beef selection and preparation. His philosophy emphasises the use of female cattle aged between six and seven years, believing this age yields a deeper, more complex flavour. Signature Dishes At the heart of Born & Bred is its basement-level speakeasy, offering an omakase-style tasting menu that showcases a progression of Hanwoo cuts: • Diamond-Cut Top Blade: Expertly grilled to highlight its marbling and tenderness. • Just-Seared Sirloin: Paired with pickled mustard seeds to complement the beef’s richness. • Hanwoo Tataki: Lightly seared and served with truffle soy sauce and yuzu peel, offering a balance of umami and citrus notes. Each course is thoughtfully curated, providing diners with a comprehensive exploration of Hanwoo’s diverse textures and flavours. Grilling Technique Born & Bred employs traditional Korean charcoal grilling methods, using binchotan to achieve precise heat control. This technique imparts a subtle smokiness, enhancing the natural flavours of the beef without overpowering them. Ambience and Experience The restaurant’s design exudes sophistication, with leather seating and bronze accents creating an intimate atmosphere reminiscent of a refined gentleman’s club. The basement speakeasy accommodates only seven guests, ensuring personalised service and an immersive dining journey. Conclusion Born & Bred offers an unparalleled Hanwoo experience, seamlessly blending traditional butchery expertise with modern culinary artistry. Under Jeong Sang-won’s guidance, the restaurant has earned its place among Asia’s top dining destinations, providing a must-visit experience for meat connoisseurs.

restaurants.name: FIREDOOR
restaurants.notes: Wood-Fired Purity and Excellence by Lennox Hastie Nestled in the heart of Surry Hills, Firedoor our former No. 1 of the ranking stands as a unique culinary establishment where every dish is prepared exclusively over wood fire. Under the guidance of Chef Lennox Hastie, the restaurant offers a dining experience that emphasises simplicity and the natural flavours of premium ingredients. Chef Lennox Hastie, renowned for his expertise in wood-fired cooking, brings a wealth of experience to Firedoor. His approach focuses on using various types of wood to enhance the inherent qualities of each ingredient, creating dishes that are both flavorful and authentic. Signature Dishes Firedoor’s menu is dynamic, changing daily to reflect seasonal availability. Notable offerings include: • Dry-Aged Rib-Eye: A meticulously 200+ days aged cut, grilled over wood fire to achieve a perfect balance of tenderness and flavour. The restaurant employs a variety of woods, such as mallee root and ironbark, to impart distinct flavours to each dish. This method ensures that the essence of the ingredients remains at the forefront, offering a dining experience that is both rustic and refined. Firedoor’s interior features an open kitchen design, allowing guests to observe the cooking process. The warm, inviting atmosphere is complemented by the aromas of wood smoke and the visual appeal of flames, creating a multisensory dining experience. Firedoor offers a distinctive approach to dining, where the focus is on the purity of ingredients and the art of wood-fired cooking. Chef Lennox Hastie’s dedication to this craft results in a menu that is both innovative and grounded in tradition.

restaurants.name: LA TÊTE D’OR
restaurants.notes: La Tête d’Or - Daniel Boulud’s Franco-American Steak restaurant in New York In November 2024, renowned French chef Daniel Boulud unveiled La Tête d’Or, his first dedicated steak restaurant, situated at One Madison Avenue in Manhattan’s Flatiron District. The restaurant’s name, translating to “The Golden Head,” pays homage to Lyon’s Parc de la Tête d’Or, reflecting Boulud’s French heritage. The establishment exudes opulence with its Art Deco influences, featuring walnut wood flooring, silk-look upholstered walls and a grand dining room adorned with brown marble and blue velvet accents . While Chef Boulud oversees the culinary direction, the kitchen’s day-to-day operations are managed by his trusted deputies, Mitchell Lienhard and Andreas Seidel. Together, they deliver a menu that marries classic American steak restaurant traditions with refined French culinary techniques . La Tête d’Or offers a diverse selection fine steak cuts, each complemented by a variety of French sauces, compound butters and surf-and-turf options. A standout is the prime rib, slow-roasted for eight hours and served tableside from a trolley, accompanied by traditional sides like creamed spinach and pommes purées. The majority of steaks are grilled over oak, imparting a distinct smoky flavour that enhances the meat’s natural richness. The open kitchen, a central feature of the restaurant, showcases the artistry involved in this traditional cooking method . La Tête d’Or stands as a testament to Daniel Boulud’s ability to seamlessly blend French culinary finesse with American steakhouse traditions. With its meticulously crafted menu, elegant design and exceptional service, the restaurant offers a dining experience that is both classic and contemporary.

restaurants.name: VICTOR CHURCHILL
restaurants.notes: Victor Churchill Melbourne – Where Butchery Becomes Theatre and Dining an Artform In the neighbourhood of Armadale, Victor Churchill Melbourne has, by 2025, firmly established itself as one of Australia’s most refined and distinctive dining destinations. Extending the legacy of the iconic Sydney butcher, this restaurant continues to blur the lines between artisanal butchery and high-end gastronomy—with breathtaking precision and undeniable flair. The space itself remains a visual masterpiece. Inside, old-world opulence meets modern minimalism, with rich timber finishes, dramatic lighting and striking glass-walled dry-ageing chambers that display prime cuts like fine jewellery. The design is not merely for show—it is an essential part of the narrative. Here, the appreciation of meat begins long before the plate. The menu is a curated showcase of exceptional provenance and technique. In 2025, signature offerings such as the 9+ Stone Axe Wagyu sirloin, the Rangers Valley Black Bistecca and the Kurobuta pork porchetta remain centrepieces, each prepared to highlight the natural flavour and texture of the product. The dry-ageing programme, now even more developed, lends depth and complexity to each bite—every steak a moment of balance between smoke, salt and buttery tenderness. Smaller plates are far from an afterthought. The Wagyu bresaola, veal tartare and Jamon Ibérico puff are executed with finesse, bridging butchery with fine dining sensibilities. Seasonal vegetables, bone marrow sides and potato variations provide texture and lift, while never distracting from the main event: the meat. The service team operates with quiet confidence and deep knowledge, offering diners insights into each breed, ageing method and preparation style. Their genuine passion elevates the experience from polished to personal. The wine programme, newly expanded in 2025, offers one of the most thoughtful meat-pairing selections in the country. Spanning iconic old-world appellations and bold new-world discoveries, the list is curated to enhance both richness and contrast—perfectly echoing the restaurant’s philosophy of precision and indulgence. Victor Churchill Melbourne is more than a restaurant. It is a celebration of craft, detail and heritage, seamlessly combining the worlds of butchery and haute cuisine. In 2025, it stands not only as a jewel of the Melbourne dining scene, but as one of the most uniquely expressive meat-driven restaurants globally—where every visit is both an education and an experience.

restaurants.name: CARCASSE
restaurants.notes: Carcasse in Koksijde – A Meat Lover’s Culinary Eden Located in the picturesque town of Koksijde, Carcasse remains a beacon for those devoted to the art of meat. Under the guidance of head chef Timon Michiels, the restaurant continues to be a shrine to high-quality, sustainably sourced meats, expertly woven into dishes that honour both innovation and tradition. Founded by master butcher Hendrik Dierendonck, Carcasse has long embodied a deep respect for craftsmanship, terroir and the primal allure of fire. From the moment one steps inside, the space envelops diners with its signature blend of rustic warmth and refined modernity. The décor—evoking a contemporary butcher’s atelier—reflects the restaurant’s ongoing commitment to authenticity and pureness. At the heart of the experience is the open kitchen, where Michiels and his team orchestrate each service with focused intent. Steak cuts—all aged in-house and selected with exacting standards—remain the star, presented alongside seasonal accompaniments and local produce in thoughtfully composed plates. Yet, 2025 signals a moment of quiet evolution. While the spirit and structure of Carcasse remain strong, there are subtle signs that the restaurant is still seeking to fully recapture the boldness and edge of its earlier peak years. Timon Michiels, though undeniably skilled and still brimming with potential, appears to be navigating a delicate phase—fine-tuning his voice within a concept that carries great expectation. That said, the level of craft and quality at Carcasse remains consistently high. The house philosophy—anchored in sustainability, respect for the animal and nose-to-tail precision—is as present as ever. Dishes such as dry-aged côte à l’os, slow-cooked veal shoulder, or house charcuterie continue to showcase a deep understanding of texture and depth. With a second Carcasse in the coastal town of Knokke, there is a clear sense of momentum and ambition surrounding the brand. The foundation is solid, the story compelling and the talent undeniable. Carcasse is very much a destination for meat lovers and culinary explorers—offering an experience rooted in substance, tradition and honest flavour.

restaurants.name: PORTEÑO
restaurants.notes: Porteño – Sydney’s Soulful Homage to Fire and Argentina Tucked into the heart of Surry Hills, Porteño remains one of Sydney’s most distinctive and enduring dining institutions. In 2025, this Argentinian powerhouse continues to embody the essence of asado culture, with its intoxicating blend of wood smoke, warm hospitality and unapologetically bold flavours. The space is as atmospheric as ever—vintage interiors, exposed brick and the unmistakable crackle of the Parrilla and Asador grills in full view. The restaurant pulses with energy, but never feels chaotic. Instead, it wraps guests in a kind of rustic glamour that feels equal parts Buenos Aires and bohemian Sydney. At the core of Porteño’s offering is its meat programme, led by the house Asado: a glorious array of beef cuts, pork and lamb, slow-cooked over ironbark and charcoal. The Shimo F1 Wagyu skirt, bone-in ribeye and the dry-aged pork tomahawk are particular highlights in 2025—each imbued with deep, smoky complexity and a texture that speaks to hours of patient grilling. The kitchen’s respect for animal and fire is unwavering. Starters and sides are no afterthought. The house-made tallow fried empanadas and the Mayura station Wagyu carpaccio remain beloved signatures. Fried Brussels sprouts with lentils and Grass-fed Wagyu tongue continue to delight as bold, umami-rich counterpoints to the flame-grilled mains. Seasonal salads and pickled garnishes bring brightness and lift to the table. Porteño’s wine list, as always, is a celebration of Argentinian and South American viticulture, with deep reds from Mendoza, Patagonian Pinot and crisp Torrontés offering compelling pairings across the board. The front-of-house team is approachable, informed and passionate about guiding guests through both the menu and the wine cellar. Service at Porteño is one of its secret weapons—genuinely warm, unpretentious and intuitive. Whether it’s your first visit or your fiftieth, there’s a sense that you’re being welcomed into a long-standing tradition with arms wide open. In 2025, Porteño continues to thrive not through reinvention, but through an unwavering dedication to what it does best: fire-driven, flavour-packed cooking that transports you to Argentina’s heartland—without ever leaving Sydney.

restaurants.name: CAPA
restaurants.notes: Capa – Nordic Fire, Refined by Casper Sobczyk In a city internationally renowned for its avant-garde approach to dining, Capa has carved out its own bold, distinctive niche. Located in the heart of Copenhagen, this steak restaurant, led by Chef Casper is no longer a hidden gem—it is one of the city’s most compelling destinations for modern carnivores and lovers of gastronomic craft. Sobczyk, known for his uncompromising attention to detail and his deep respect for prime ingredients working with I.e. the finest cuts of Sashi meat, has elevated Capa into something far more than a steak restaurant. His approach is rooted in Nordic seasonality, but executed with global perspective. Whether sourcing locally raised dairy cow beef for long dry-ageing or featuring rare heritage breeds from Denmark and beyond, the focus is always on maturity, marbling and method. The signature cuts—particularly the dry-aged ribeye and thick-cut tenderloin are treated with reverence, allowed to speak for themselves without excess intervention. Yet Sobczyk’s finesse is felt in the subtlety of each plate: in the house-fermented accompaniments, in the smoked butter that melts over the meat at the last moment, or in the carefully composed sauces that never overpower. Starters and sides display equal care. From bone marrow with pickled onions to seasonal root vegetables roasted in beef fat, every dish offers balance and contrast. The restaurant’s house charcuterie programme continues to grow, reflecting its dedication to whole animal usage and sustainability. The experience is immersive and unpretentious, guided by a service team that is attentive, confident and grounded in genuine hospitality. The sommelier team is knowledgeable without being imposing, always ready to match the nuance of the grill with equal weight in the glass. In 2025, Capa under Casper Sobczyk’s leadership represents the future of European steakhouse dining: conscious and deeply rooted in technique. It is not just a celebration of beef—but of Nordic clarity, culinary intellect and understated power.

restaurants.name: GILLIS
restaurants.notes: Gillis – Ghent’s Meat-Driven Masterpiece Continues Its Ascent In the heart of Belgium’s ever-evolving culinary landscape, Gillis in Ghent has firmly established itself as one of the country’s most compelling dining destinations. Just steps away from the historic city centre, this modern steakhouse—guided by the vision of chef-owner Bram Candries—has once again climbed the ranks in the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants, earning recognition for its exceptional performance in 2025. Gillis offers a refreshingly contemporary interpretation of Flemish cuisine, where time-honoured techniques and regional ingredients are elevated by a refined, modern aesthetic. The atmosphere strikes a careful balance between urban elegance and rustic charm, with exposed wood, soft lighting and industrial touches creating a space that feels both intimate and effortlessly stylish. The kitchen operates around a sharing concept—generous, beautifully composed plates designed for communal indulgence. Signature highlights include the hand-cut Holstein beef tartare, a dish of texture and clarity; the slow-cooked Duroc pork belly, rich, tender and full of umami; and the beloved pork cheek croquettes, served with a tangy Belgian pickle mayonnaise that bridges tradition and innovation in one perfect bite. Candries’ commitment to radical quality is evident in every aspect of the menu, from meat sourcing to seasoning. Steaks come from carefully selected producers across Europe, including aged cuts that are grilled to perfection and carved for the table. There is confidence in the simplicity—no excess, just respect for the product. The wine list, curated by Bram himself and executed with polish by the sommelier team, reflects a deep understanding of pairing and provenance. With an emphasis on Old World classics, the list includes standout Burgundy, Rhône and Piedmont selections, alongside hidden gems that reveal the team’s passion for soulful, structured wines. Service is professional yet warm, led by Jozefien, Bram’s wife and the restaurant’s gracious front-of-house presence. The staff combine sharp attention to detail with a relaxed, genuine hospitality that puts guests at ease from the moment they arrive. In 2025, Gillis is not just a place to eat—it’s a place to experience Flemish culinary culture reimagined through excellence, sharing and uncompromising quality. With a clear identity, a devoted following and its highest-ever ranking so far, Gillis continues to raise the bar - not just for Ghent, but for Belgium.

restaurants.name: HAWKSMOOR
restaurants.notes: Hawksmoor Air Street – A Celebration of British Grass-Fed Beef in the Heart of London Located just off Regent Street, Hawksmoor Air Street combines the grandeur of Art Deco design with a menu that pays homage to the finest British beef and sustainable seafood. The restaurant’s interior features sweeping ceilings and stained-glass windows, creating an elegant yet welcoming atmosphere. At the core of Hawksmoor’s culinary philosophy helmed by Group Executive Chef Matt Brown is a commitment to grass-fed, traditionally reared British cattle. Their beef is sourced from small farms across the UK, where cattle are raised on a natural diet of grass and hay, leading to meat that is rich in flavour and tenderness. Each cut is dry-aged for 35 days to enhance its depth and character. The menu offers a variety of steak options, including porterhouse, bone-in prime rib and ribeye, all cooked to perfection. Complementing the beef are carefully selected sides and sauces, such as bone marrow gravy and anchovy hollandaise. In addition to its renowned steaks, Hawksmoor Air Street also features a seafood selection developed in collaboration with chef Mitch Tonks. Dishes like whole native lobster and grilled Dover sole showcase the restaurant’s dedication to quality and sustainability. The beverage program includes a thoughtfully curated wine list and a selection of expertly crafted cocktails, enhancing the overall dining experience. Whether you’re a local or a visitor, Hawksmoor Air Street offers a quintessential British dining experience that celebrates the country’s rich culinary heritage. Hawksmoor also holds the number 2 position in our global ranking of restaurants with multiple outlets.

restaurants.name: BRAT
restaurants.notes: London’s Temple of Wood-Fired Simplicity Perched above a former Shoreditch strip club, Brat has swiftly become one of London’s most celebrated dining destinations. Helmed by Welsh chef Tomos Parry, the restaurant stands as a testament to its distinctive approach to cooking over fire. Chef Tomos, drawing inspiration from his Welsh heritage and the Basque region of northern Spain, has crafted a menu that emphasises simplicity and quality. His philosophy centres on letting exceptional ingredients speak for themselves, a principle evident in every dish served at Brat. While Brat is renowned for its signature whole turbot, its meat offerings, particularly the steak cuts, are equally fantastic. Chef Tomos Parry’s commitment to sourcing high-quality steak cuts from leading selected Britsh farms like the i.e. Hereford Sirloin and the Angus Rib and his expertise in wood-fired cooking culminate in dishes that are both flavourful and expertly prepared. Central to Brat’s culinary DNA is its use of a wood-fired grill. This approach imparts a unique smoky flavour to the dishes, creating a dining experience that is both rustic and refined . The dining room exudes a warm, inviting atmosphere, with large industrial windows allowing natural light to flood the space. The open kitchen design enables guests to witness the artistry of wood-fired cooking firsthand, adding an element of theatre to the meal. Brat stands as a testament to the power of simplicity and the allure of cooking with fire. Chef Tomos Parry’s dedication to quality ingredients and traditional techniques has solidified the restaurant’s status as a must-visit for those seeking an authentic and memorable dining experience in London.

restaurants.name: LA BRASERIA
restaurants.notes: La Braseria – Where Italian Meat Culture Meets Culinary Artistry Nestled in the heart of Osio Sotto, just outside Bergamo, La Braseria has firmly established itself as one of Italy’s premier destinations for meat enthusiasts. Under the guidance of Chef Luca Brasi, this establishment masterfully combines traditional Italian meat craftsmanship with contemporary culinary innovation. A Temple to Italian Meat La Braseria’s philosophy centers on the meticulous selection and preparation of meat. The restaurant sources the finest local Italian breeds, including Italian Wagyu and Piedmontese Fat Ox, ensuring each cut is of the highest quality. These meats undergo a self-managed dry-aging process, tailored to the specific breed and age of the animal, enhancing flavor and tenderness. Culinary Excellence Chef Brasi’s menu is a celebration of Italian culinary tradition, featuring dishes that highlight the natural flavours of the meat. Signature offerings include the tortelli di mandorle amare con tartufo nero bergamasco, a dish that showcases the chef’s ability to blend classic ingredients with modern techniques. The restaurant’s commitment to sustainability and tradition is evident in every dish, with a focus on seasonal, locally sourced ingredients. An Inviting Ambiance The restaurant’s interior exudes rustic charm, with exposed brick walls and wooden beams creating a cozy, old-world atmosphere. Guests can observe the ageing process of the meats through on-site dry ageing cabinets, emphasising the restaurant’s transparency and dedication to quality. The professional and attentive staff enhance the dining experience, offering expert recommendations on both dishes and wine pairings. La Braseria is a testament to Italy’s rich meat culture and culinary heritage. For those seeking an authentic and unforgettable dining experience, La Braseria in Osio Sotto stands as a beacon of excellence.

restaurants.name: ELISA
restaurants.notes: Elisa – Vancouver’s Modern Temple to Fire and Precision In the heart of Yaletown, Vancouver’s stylish culinary district, Elisa continues to set the benchmark for modern steak restaurant dining in Canada. In 2025, this wood-fired destination blends architectural elegance with culinary integrity, delivering an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. The restaurant’s design—warm, open and theatrically centred around its custom-built wood-fired grill—reflects the energy of the kitchen, where flame and flavour work in perfect harmony. Under the leadership of Executive Chef Andrew Richardson, Elisa refines the art of grilling with exacting care and a steadfast commitment to product quality. The steak programme remains at the heart of the experience, showcasing a world-class portfolio of beef. From Canadian and US Prime cuts to highly sought-after Japanese Wagyu, each piece is cooked over sustainably sourced hardwood, imbuing it with subtle smoke and a true depth of flavour. A standout in 2025 is the Alberta Black Angus bone-in ribeye—charred beautifully, tender throughout and boasting a richness that lingers long after the final bite. The menu balances opulence with restraint. Accompanying dishes, such as the branzino with cavolo nero or the house favourite crispy hashbrowns, are executed with polish and intention. Triple-fried chips, seasonal greens and indulgent sauces like béarnaise and chimichurri round out the offering with classic flair. Elisa’s wine programme continues to impress, with an expertly curated list that pays equal respect to local British Columbia vineyards and global benchmarks. Whether guests seek structured reds or elegant whites, the sommelier team ensures thoughtful pairings that complement every cut and course. Service is where Elisa truly excels. The front-of-house team balances polished professionalism with natural warmth, moving fluidly between knowledgeable recommendations and attentive, unobtrusive care. Every table is treated like a returning guest—even on a first visit. In 2025, Elisa is more than just Canada’s best steak restaurant - it’s a destination where fire cooking becomes refined ritual. It remains Canada’s reference point for wood-fired dining at scale and a must-visit for anyone who believes steak should be both an experience and an art form.

restaurants.name: NIKUYA TANAKA
restaurants.notes: Tucked away in the heart of Ginza, Nikuya Tanaka offers an intimate and refined dining experience that celebrates the pinnacle of Japanese beef cuisine. Founded and helmed by Chef Satoru Tanaka, affectionately known as “Boss,” the restaurant has garnered acclaim for its innovative approach to wagyu-focused kappo dining. Chef Satoru Tanaka, a third-generation meat specialist, brings a lifetime of expertise to his craft. His deep understanding of beef, honed over years of experience, is evident in every course. Chef Tanaka’s philosophy emphasizes the natural flavours of the meat, often employing minimalist techniques to highlight its inherent qualities. Nikuya Tanaka’s menu is a testament to the versatility and depth of wagyu beef: • Wagyu Tartare: Expertly seasoned and presented, showcasing the purity and richness of the beef. • Charcoal-Grilled Sirloin: Meticulously grilled over binchotan charcoal, imparting a distinctive smoky flavour that enhances the meat’s natural taste. Each course is thoughtfully curated to provide a harmonious progression of flavours and textures, ensuring a memorable dining journey. Central to Nikuya Tanaka’s culinary philosophy is its commitment to traditional grilling methods. The restaurant employs a binchotan charcoal grill, allowing Chef Tanaka to impart a distinctive smoky flavour to his dishes. This method enhances the natural taste of the premium wagyu cuts, providing a depth of flavour that is both authentic and refined. The restaurant’s interior exudes understated elegance, featuring a Hinoki (Japanese cypress) counter that seats up to eight guests, fostering an intimate atmosphere. This setting allows diners to witness the meticulous preparation of each dish, adding a theatrical element to the dining experience. Nikuya Tanaka stands as a beacon of culinary excellence in Tokyo, seamlessly blending traditional kappo principles with a modern emphasis on premium wagyu beef. Chef Satoru Tanaka’s dedication to his craft ensures that each visit offers a unique and unforgettable gastronomic experience.

restaurants.name: LOS 33
restaurants.notes: Los 33 – Where Fire, Heritage and clear Visions Ignite Tucked into the lively streets of the Salesas district, Los 33 is a culinary gem that seamlessly fuses Spanish tradition with South American fire, under the confident and creative direction of Chef Oswaldo. With its warm yet contemporary atmosphere, the restaurant has quickly become a reference point for those seeking bold, soulful flavours delivered with finesse. Chef Oswaldo’s culinary identity is rooted in the parrilla tradition, but his menu is far more than just grilled meat—it’s a refined celebration of both Iberian and Latin American influences, crafted with precision and an eye for detail. His signature Galician Rubia steak, dry-aged for 60 days, is a showstopper: rich, earthy and deeply satisfying. Cooked over open embers, it captures the essence of wood-fired perfection. Equally impressive are the starters and small bites, where Oswaldo’s technique shines in more delicate expressions. The veal sweetbreads, crisp on the outside and melting within, are among the best in the world. Ham croquettes and the grilled Bikini sandwich deliver comfort and nostalgia with gourmet precision—each bite layered with texture and intent. The menu flows with purpose, honouring Spanish produce and South American soul. The sides and accompaniments—think charred seasonal vegetables, piquant chimichurri, or silky purées—provide both balance and elevation, making the meal feel complete yet never overworked. The wine list at Los 33 is as thoughtful as the food. Predominantly Spanish in its structure, it also ventures into Latin American and Old World territories with intention. The staff are not only attentive and gracious but clearly passionate—ready to suggest a Ribera del Duero or a bold Tannat to accompany the heat and complexity of the grill. Service is polished yet personal, with a team that moves fluidly between professionalism and warmth. Whether you’re seated at the bar for a quick bite or settling in for a long evening around the fire, you’re in capable, confident hands. In 2025, Los 33 continues to distinguish itself—not only as one of Madrid’s most engaging dining experiences, but as a place where the vision of the owner and power couple Nacho Ventosa and Sara Aznar turns tradition into something both exciting and enduring. It’s a restaurant where every plate tells a story of fire, craft and cultural convergence.

restaurants.name: REGINA BISTECCA
restaurants.notes: Regina Bistecca – A Refined Tribute to Tuscan Tradition in the Heart of Florence Tucked just steps from the Duomo in Florence’s historic centre, Regina Bistecca continues to capture the essence of Tuscan gastronomy in 2025 with poise, tradition and a refined edge. Housed in what was once part of the city’s famed anatomy theatre, the restaurant offers a space that feels steeped in history, where exposed brickwork, antique bookshelves and vintage chandeliers create an atmosphere that’s both cultured and unmistakably Florentine. At the heart of the menu lies the bistecca alla Fiorentina, the undisputed star of the show. Sourced exclusively from Chianina IGP cattle, this signature cut is grilled over a wood-fired hearth, expertly charred on the outside while remaining wonderfully rare and juicy within. Seasoned with nothing more than coarse salt, freshly ground pepper and a drizzle of Tuscan olive oil, the steak is a study in restraint, allowing the full character of the meat to shine. In 2025, the kitchen continues to respect tradition while subtly evolving. Starters such as chicken liver crostini and hand-cut veal tartare add elegance to the opening act, while the seasonal sides—including creamed spinach with Parmigiano and Fagioli Zolfini with extra-virgin olive oil—offer balance and warmth in every bite. The wine list is as thoughtfully assembled as ever, with a strong focus on Tuscan reds—from structured Brunello di Montalcino to robust Chianti Classico Riserva—alongside a curated selection of Super Tuscans and lesser-known regional gems. The sommelier team is engaging and insightful, helping guests navigate the offerings with charm and confidence. Service at Regina Bistecca remains a standout feature. The staff are attentive yet discreet, embodying the Italian spirit of welcoming hospitality with elegant professionalism. Their deep knowledge of the dishes and wines adds an enriching narrative to the experience. In 2025, Regina Bistecca continues to be Florence’s most reliable destinations for lovers of authentic bistecca alla Fiorentina, offering an experience that’s equal parts culinary tradition, visual beauty and heartfelt hospitality. It’s a must-visit for anyone seeking to understand the true soul of Tuscan cuisine, delivered with style and substance.

restaurants.name: TRIBUTO
restaurants.street: Isabel la Católica N24-54 entre Luis Cordero y Francisco Salazar
restaurants.notes: A Celebration of Fire, Origin and Ecuadorian Culinary Identity Tributo, helmed by chef-owner Luis Maldonado continues to distinguish itself in 2025 as one of Ecuador’s most compelling dining destinations. Rooted in the ethos of “Fuego y Origen”, this restaurant offers more than just a meal—it delivers a visceral, deeply personal homage to Ecuadorian culinary heritage, interpreted through contemporary technique and elemental fire. The ambiance at Tributo exudes modern elegance, layered with authentic warmth. Earth-toned woods, soft golden lighting and curated artistic details create a space that feels both refined and deeply grounded in Andean culture. It is a setting where tradition and innovation sit comfortably side by side—equally suited to intimate evenings or celebratory feasts. The menu is fearless and focused, built on a commitment to nose-to-tail cooking and hyper-local sourcing. At its centre is the now-iconic Txuletón de Vaca—a 120-day dry-aged cut from an old cow raised at 4,000 metres in the Ecuadorian Andes. Rich, marbled and deeply flavourful, it’s a carnivore’s epiphany and a true expression of high-altitude terroir. The in-house charcuterie programme is equally impressive, showcasing meticulous aging and technique. Other highlights include the “sancocho de la abuela Rosa”, a 48-hour smoked rib broth layered with depth and nostalgia and the tostada de lengua ahumada, where smoked beef tongue is elevated with precision and soul. Each dish feels rooted in memory, yet forward-thinking in execution. The cocktail programme deserves its own mention—playful yet sophisticated, built around native botanicals, herbs and regional spirits. From canelazo-infused aperitifs to wild fruit macerations, the drinks reflect the same dedication to Ecuadorian identity that drives the kitchen. Service is personal and polished, delivered by a team that is both passionate and deeply knowledgeable. The sommelier, always close at hand, guides diners through a well-curated wine list that spans both expressive South American producers and classic Old World vintages—each pairing designed to enhance the restaurant’s bold, fire-driven flavours. In 2025, Tributo stands not only as a must-visit in Quito, but as a flagbearer for modern Ecuadorian gastronomy. With its fusion of fire, origin and creative precision, it offers an experience that is as meaningful as it is memorable—an invitation to rediscover Ecuador through flame and flavour.

restaurants.name: AMAREN
restaurants.notes: A Basque Steakhouse Elevating Fire, Heritage and Elegance in Bilbao In the lively heart of Bilbao, Amaren continues to stand out in 2025 as one of the city’s most essential dining destinations—where the ancient art of Basque grilling meets contemporary finesse. Under the direction of Chef Aitor, Amaren honours the soul of traditional txuleton cuisine while offering a dining experience that is both polished and deeply rooted. The restaurant’s interior is stylish and warm, blending modern design elements with classic Basque materials like stone, wood and iron. It’s an atmosphere that feels both intimate and vibrant—welcoming locals and travellers alike, whether for a relaxed evening out or a celebratory occasion. At the centre of Amaren’s offering is its exceptional beef programme. Specialising in carefully selected old cows and oxen from the Iberian Peninsula, the restaurant’s steaks are grilled with reverence, revealing deep, earthy flavours and sublime texture. Every cut is treated with the utmost respect—from ageing to the final moment on the fire—showcasing Aitor’s mastery and the Basque region’s rich cattle heritage. But Amaren is more than a steakhouse. The menu reflects a wider celebration of local ingredients and seasonal creativity, with dishes that honour the region’s traditions while subtly incorporating modern technique. The wine list in 2025 continues to impress, offering a dynamic range from Basque and Spanish appellations to Old World classics, each pairing crafted to elevate the bold, flame-grilled cuisine. The knowledgeable front-of-house team is attentive yet relaxed, always ready to guide guests through the experience with care and quiet enthusiasm. In 2025, Amaren is not only a reference point for meat lovers in Bilbao—it’s a culinary expression of Basque identity, fire and thoughtful innovation. With its balance of flavour, authenticity and professionalism, it is a must-visit destination for anyone eager to explore the deeper dimensions of northern Spain’s vibrant food scene.

restaurants.name: THE GIDLEY
restaurants.notes: The Gidley - Sydney’s Modern Tribute to Old-World Steakhouse Glamour Hidden beneath the streets of Sydney’s bustling CBD, The Gidley continues to set the standard in 2025 for timeless steak restaurant elegance with a distinctly modern Australian flair. With its dark wood panelling, vintage lighting and plush leather booths, the restaurant channels the intimate sophistication of a bygone era—like stepping into a well-kept secret reserved for those who know where to look. The Gidley’s atmosphere remains one of its most alluring traits. It is cosy yet grand, luxurious but never pretentious. The layout encourages both conviviality and privacy, making it ideal for romantic dinners, business evenings, or slow, indulgent lunches where time feels wonderfully suspended. At the heart of the experience is the beef—and in 2025, The Gidley continues to shine with its commitment to premium Australian cuts. The Riverine Black Angus ribeye remains a showstopper: rich, perfectly marbled and flame-kissed to a precise medium-rare, achieving that ideal balance of char and succulence. Other cuts, including the bone-in sirloin and rib on the bone, are equally impressive—each prepared with the kind of exacting technique that reflects serious culinary craftsmanship. Side dishes are elevated with care and creativity. The creamed spinach is lush without being heavy, while the hand-cut fries are golden and crisp, served simply but executed perfectly. The bone marrow with truffle jus continues to seduce those seeking something deeper, more decadent and unapologetically indulgent. The wine list is a serious affair. Curated with intent, it showcases both bold Australian vintages and refined Old World classics, offering options for both the traditionalist and the curious. Staff are impressively knowledgeable—always ready with a pairing suggestion that brings the best out of both wine and dish, without ever overwhelming the guest. Service is where The Gidley truly excels. The front-of-house team balances professionalism with personality—attentive, informed and charming, creating a dining experience that feels tailored without being stiff. In 2025, The Gidley is not merely a steakhouse - it’s a celebration of refinement, warmth and the art of excellent hospitality. For those in search of a dining experience that feels both indulgent and impeccably curated, it remains a must-visit jewel in Sydney’s culinary crown.

restaurants.name: BEEFBAR
restaurants.notes: Beefbar Monaco – A Modern Icon of Meat Culture Nestled in the heart of Monte Carlo, Beefbar Monaco stands as the flagship of Riccardo Giraudi’s global restaurant empire. Since its inception in 2005, this establishment has redefined the traditional steakhouse, blending contemporary luxury with a cosmopolitan flair. The culinary experience at Beefbar Monaco is orchestrated by Executive Chef Thierry Paludetto, who collaborates closely with Giraudi to craft a menu that celebrates simplicity and flavour. Their offerings range from street food-inspired dishes to premium cuts of meat sourced from renowned producers in Australia, Japan and the United States. Notably, Giraudi holds exclusive rights to import Japanese Kobe beef into Europe, ensuring an unparalleled dining experience. The restaurant’s ambiance reflects its innovative spirit, featuring sleek architectural designs that create an inviting yet sophisticated atmosphere. This commitment to excellence has earned Beefbar Monaco accolades not only as an individual restaurant listed in our ranking but also as the No. 1 steak restaurant brand with multiple locations for the third time in a row now. In essence, Beefbar Monaco is more than just a dining venue - it’s a testament to Giraudi’s vision of modernising meat culture, offering guests an unforgettable journey through taste and elegance.

restaurants.name: ANAHI
restaurants.notes: Latin Soul, Parisian Elegance and Fire-Lit Precision Tucked into the vibrant Marais district, Anahi is a culinary revival unlike any other in Paris. Co-helmed by Mauro Colagreco, the celebrated Argentine chef behind Mirazur and Riccardo Giraudi, the visionary behind the global Beefbar empire, Anahi represents a seamless blend of South American spirit, French sophistication and fire-driven meat mastery. Set within a former butcher shop, the space retains its heritage—mosaic tiles, ironwork and mirrored ceilings—while exuding warmth and intimacy. In 2025, it remains one of Paris’s most atmospheric dining rooms: chic but soulful, refined yet rebellious. At its core, Anahi celebrates premium cuts of beef, prepared with reverence and restraint. The menu draws from Colagreco’s Argentine roots, Giraudi’s unparalleled access to rare breeds and exclusive meats and the French capital’s insatiable appetite for quality. From Rubia Gallega côte de bœuf to Kobe and American Wagyu, each steak is grilled to smoky perfection over open flame, allowing the natural texture and marbling of the meat to take centre stage. But Anahi is much more than a steak restaurant. It’s a place where Latin flavours meet Parisian finesse—where empanadas arrive golden and delicate, chorizo and provoleta are reimagined with nuance and tangy chimichurri or smoky bone marrow elevate every plate with thoughtful precision. The wine and cocktail programme adds another layer of intrigue. A curated list of bold Malbecs, elegant Bordeaux and boutique South American finds is complemented by a bar offering house-aged Negronis and Pisco-based creations—each drink crafted with the same elegance as the food. Service at Anahi is deeply personal. The team moves with rhythm and warmth, offering knowledge without pretense and guiding guests through both the menu and its ethos—an ethos grounded in heritage, fire and celebration. In 2025, Anahi stands as one of Paris’s most stylish and compelling dining destinations. For those seeking a steak experience that transcends tradition, anchored by two global powerhouses of cuisine, Anahi is not only relevant—it’s essential.

restaurants.name: FOGÓN
restaurants.notes: Fogón Asado – A Contemporary Ode to Argentina’s Asado Tradition Nestled in the vibrant heart of Palermo, Buenos Aires, Fogón Asado redefines the traditional Argentine parrilla, offering an intimate and interactive culinary journey that celebrates the rich heritage of asado with modern sophistication. Fogón Asado invites guests to a unique dining experience centered around a U-shaped chef’s counter, accommodating just twelve patrons. This setup fosters direct engagement with the chefs, who expertly prepare each course over a wood-fired grill, sharing insights into the origins of the ingredients and the nuances of Argentine grilling techniques. The meticulously curated tasting menu showcases a progression of flavours and textures, beginning with delicate starters like grilled provoleta cheese paired with pear and advancing to robust cuts such as smoked ribeye cap and slow-cooked beef. Each dish is thoughtfully presented, reflecting a balance between tradition and innovation. Complementing the culinary offerings is a selection of Argentine wines, carefully chosen to enhance the flavours of each course. The knowledgeable staff provides personalised recommendations, ensuring a harmonious pairing that elevates the dining experience. Fogón Asado stands as a testament to Argentina’s culinary evolution, delivering an unforgettable experience that marries the communal spirit of the asado with contemporary fine dining. For those seeking an authentic yet innovative taste of Argentine cuisine, this establishment is a must-visit destination. Fogón will also be the exclusive host restaurant of The Rare Tour 2025 in Argentina welcoming I Due Cippi from Tuscany - a Top 5 restaurant of the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants - for a 4-hands dinner experience.

restaurants.name: AALIA
restaurants.notes: A Contemporary Ode to Middle Eastern Grilling in Sydney Situated in the heart of Sydney’s CBD at 25 Martin Place, AALIA redefines Middle Eastern cuisine through a modern lens. Under the leadership of Executive Chef Paul Farag, the restaurant delves into the rich tapestry of Middle Eastern and North African culinary traditions, bringing to light lesser-known ingredients and ancient cooking techniques. Chef Paul Farag’s dedication to authenticity is evident in his meticulous research, which includes studying 10th-century Arabic cookbooks to resurrect forgotten recipes. His approach marries historical depth with contemporary innovation, offering diners a unique gastronomic journey. AALIA’s menu is a celebration of meat-centric dishes that pay homage to traditional Middle Eastern flavours while embracing modern culinary techniques which makes this restaurant special and unique. We would like to highlight an excellent Kiwami 9+ flank steak (we love secondary cuts), cooked perfectly to medium-rare and served with an irresistible Café de Cairo sauce. Central to AALIA’s culinary philosophy is its commitment to traditional grilling methods. The restaurant employs an ironbark wood and charcoal grill, sustainably sourced from the Blue Mountains, to impart a distinctive smoky flavour to its meats. Designed by architect Matt Darwon, AALIA’s interior seamlessly blends contemporary aesthetics with Middle Eastern influences. The space features sculptural wooden elements and warm lighting, creating an inviting atmosphere that complements the culinary offerings. AALIA stands as a testament to the rich culinary heritage of the Middle East, reimagined through a modern Australian perspective. With its focus on authentic flavours, traditional grilling techniques and elegant ambiance, AALIA offers a dining experience that is both rooted in history and refreshingly contemporary.

restaurants.name: STEER DINING ROOM
