restaurants.notes: Steer Dining Room – Melbourne’s Precision-Driven Temple of Wagyu Excellence In the heart of South Yarra, Steer Dining Room continues to set the benchmark in 2025 for steakhouse dining in Melbourne. Seamlessly blending urban refinement with culinary innovation, this stylish venue has evolved into a serious destination for Wagyu connoisseurs and lovers of modern luxury alike. The space is sleek and sophisticated - minimalist design softened by warm lighting and tactile textures, offering an ambiance that feels both exclusive and inviting. Whether for a celebratory occasion or a midweek indulgence, Steer strikes that elusive balance between high-end polish and relaxed approachability. At the centre of the experience is one of Australia’s most comprehensive Wagyu programmes, featuring an exceptional array of local and international cuts, meticulously sourced and clearly categorised by breed, origin, marble score and ageing. The A5 Kobe Wagyu remains a showstopper - its silken texture and intense umami coaxed to perfection through the chefs’ expert touch. Equally impressive is the Stone Axe Wagyu tenderloin revealing the nuanced differences between producers and regions. While beef is undoubtedly the star, the supporting cast is equally thoughtful. Dishes such as the prawn Katsu sandwich or the Wagyu tataki with yuzu kosho and sesame show off the kitchen’s versatility and restraint. Everything is built around precision, balance and an uncompromising respect for flavour. The wine list is expansive, with a curated focus on bold Australian reds, rare Old World classics and structured whites—each selected with steak pairings in mind. The sommelier team is passionate and well-versed, always ready to guide you through both familiar and adventurous territory. Service remains one of Steer’s great strengths. The team is polished yet personable, attentive without intruding and clearly proud to be part of one of Melbourne’s most elevated meat-focused experiences. Steer Dining Room continues to lead not by spectacle, but by discipline, sourcing and quiet confidence. For those who appreciate steak as a craft, not just a cut, Steer is an essential Melbourne institution.

restaurants.name: 11WOODFIRE
restaurants.notes: Nestled within a sleek villa on Jumeirah Beach Road, 11 Woodfire stands as a testament to the primal allure of open-fire cooking. The restaurant offers a dining experience that marries rustic charm with refined culinary artistry. The talented Head Chef at 11 Woodfire in Dubai is Brando Moros. Chef Moros, originally from Colombia, brings over 15 years of culinary experience to the restaurant. His expertise in wood-fired grilling and his philosophy of “elevating simplicity” are central to the dining experience at 11 Woodfire. Chef Moros leads the kitchen’s focus on traditional wood fire cooking, utilising a hand-built wood-burning oven and a forged steel grill. This method employs fire, smoke, wood and blended embers to extract natural flavors from carefully selected seafood, prime meats, grains and vegetables. The menu at 11 Woodfire is a curated selection of globally sourced ingredients, each treated with reverence over flames fueled by oak, hickory, or hay. The restaurant’s interior exudes a minimalist elegance, with an open kitchen that invites diners to witness the culinary theatre of flames and smoke. The atmosphere is both relaxed and refined, making it suitable for intimate dinners and social gatherings alike. 11 Woodfire is more than a dining destination; it’s an exploration of flavours ignited by fire and guided by culinary expertise. For those seeking an unforgettable gastronomic experience in Dubai, this establishment delivers on all fronts.

restaurants.name: LUTYENS GRILL
restaurants.notes: Timeless Elegance and Elevated Steaks at The Ned Tucked within the storied walls of The Ned in the heart of London’s financial district, Lutyens Grill continues to affirm its place among the capital’s most refined steak destinations. The dining room remains a study in quiet luxury: dark wood panelling, plush leather chairs, soft lighting and the unmistakable charm of an old-world London club. Yet despite its historical gravitas, Lutyens Grill never feels dated—it strikes a perfect balance between tradition and contemporary ease. The menu continues to revolve around exceptional rare breed cuts, sourced with precision and grilled to perfection. Standout options include the Hereford chateaubriand for two, served with theatrical flair; the richly marbled Spanish ox chop, robust in flavour; and the perfectly tender fillet steak that epitomises classic indulgence. Cuts are carved tableside from the elegant trolley, lending a touch of ceremony to each meal. Beyond the beef, the kitchen impresses with dishes that echo the same meticulous attention to craft. Signature plates such as the lobster Thermidor, the hand-cut steak tartare and the indulgent chocolate mud pie bring balance and sophistication to the menu. Seasonal sides and refined sauces provide the perfect complement to the grill. Service, led by the ever-gracious Giancarlo Cuccuru, remains one of Lutyens Grill’s most defining strengths. The team is discreet yet attentive, offering confident wine pairings and warm, personalised hospitality that elevates the experience from fine to unforgettable. In 2025, Lutyens Grill is more than a hidden gem—it is a destination of culinary heritage, quiet confidence and world-class steak expertise. Its continued ascent in global rankings reflects not only the strength of its offering, but the soul with which it’s delivered. For those seeking an oasis of flavour and elegance in the City of London, Lutyens Grill is a must.

restaurants.name: MIYOSHI
restaurants.notes: Kyoto’s Gem of Wagyu Kaiseki Mastery Tucked away in the historic Gion district of Kyoto, Miyoshi offers an intimate and refined dining experience that celebrates the art of Japanese beef cuisine. Under the meticulous guidance of Chef Tsutomu Ito, the restaurant has garnered acclaim for its innovative approach to wagyu-focused kaiseki dining. Chef Tsutomu Ito, a self-taught culinary artisan, draws inspiration from his profound appreciation for premium Japanese beef. His dedication to sourcing the finest cuts and his commitment to seasonal ingredients are evident in every course. Chef Ito’s philosophy emphasises the natural flavours of the meat, often employing minimalist techniques to highlight its inherent qualities. Signature Dishes Miyoshi’s menu is a testament to the versatility and depth of wagyu beef: • Kombu-Pressed Ox Tongue: A signature dish where the ox tongue is pressed with kelp, resulting in a luxuriously smooth texture that melts on the palate. • Beef Consommé with White Truffles: A clear, aromatic broth served in a wine glass to enhance the truffle’s fragrance, offering a delicate yet profound flavour profile. • Sirloin Sashimi: Thinly sliced, marbled sirloin presented raw, showcasing the purity and richness of the beef. Each course is thoughtfully curated to provide a harmonious progression of flavours and textures, ensuring a memorable dining journey. The restaurant’s interior exudes understated elegance, featuring a Hinoki (Japanese cypress) counter that seats up to eight guests, fostering an intimate atmosphere. A recent renovation in August 2023 introduced a private counter on the second floor, exclusively available to lodging guests, offering a bespoke dining experience. Miyoshi stands as a beacon of culinary excellence in Kyoto, seamlessly blending traditional kaiseki principles with a modern emphasis on premium wagyu beef. Chef Tsutomu Ito’s dedication to his craft ensures that each visit offers a unique and unforgettable gastronomic experience.

restaurants.name: ARAGAWA
restaurants.notes: Japanese Purity, Mayfair Elegance and Wagyu at Its Finest In 2025, Aragawa London continues to establish itself as one of the most exclusive and refined steak destinations in the city—and globally. As a new entry in 2024 in the World’s 101 Best Steak Restaurants, this Mayfair-based sanctuary of simplicity and precision has quickly become a must-visit for connoisseurs of Wagyu and those who appreciate the quiet mastery of Japanese culinary tradition. The restaurant’s ambiance mirrors the ethos of its cuisine: understated, graceful and deliberate. Clean architectural lines, soft lighting and tactile materials create an intimate space that feels removed from the bustle of central London. Every detail is curated to focus attention on the main event—the beef. At the heart of Aragawa’s philosophy is its unwavering commitment to the highest-grade Japanese Wagyu, meticulously selected and cooked over binchōtan charcoal with no more than salt and time-honoured technique. The result is Wagyu at its purest: incredibly tender, intensely marbled, yet elegant on the palate—never heavy, always balanced. The most coveted cuts, including Tajima and Kobe beef, are served in precise portions that honour the craftsmanship behind the product. The menu remains focused and minimalistic, celebrating restraint rather than opulence. Every element on the plate is intentional. Accompanying dishes—like seasonal Japanese pickles, dashi-based soups and perfectly steamed Koshihikari rice—provide thoughtful balance and structure to the richness of the meat. The sake and wine programme is curated with equal care. Whether it’s a rare Junmai Daiginjo or an aged Bordeaux, the pairings are designed to complement the delicate umami profile of the Wagyu without overshadowing it. The sommelier team and waitstaff are deeply knowledgeable, with service that is quietly confident, deeply respectful and perfectly timed. In a dining scene often defined by spectacle, Aragawa offers something more meaningful: an immersive, meditative experience built around purity, discipline and reverence for the ingredient.

restaurants.name: CLOVER GRILL
restaurants.notes: Parisian Fire, Global Beef and the Art of Simplicity Clover Grill is Chef Jean-François Piège’s fire-driven ode to meat—an elegant yet unpretentious Parisian restaurant where wood, smoke and exceptional sourcing converge to create one of the city’s finest destinations for steak lovers. In 2025, Clover Grill remains a benchmark for modern meat-focused dining in France. The dining room is chic and polished, effortlessly Parisian in its charm, with dark marble, warm wood and glowing embers setting the tone for an experience that’s both refined and primal. At the heart of Clover Grill is its remarkable meat programme. Steaks are sourced with meticulous care from the best producers in France, Spain and Japan, including Simmental, Galician Blond, Black Angus and Japanese Wagyu. Cuts are grilled either over an open flame or roasted in a Josper charcoal oven, depending on the breed and marbling, ensuring optimal texture and intensity. The côte de bœuf, particularly when sourced from mature dairy breeds or long-aged beef, is a must-try—charred on the outside, blushing in the centre and bursting with deep, savoury complexity. The filet de bœuf au poivre and entrecôte de Galice also showcase the kitchen’s mastery in coaxing out maximum flavour while maintaining elegance in presentation. Sides and starters reflect Piège’s classical training and minimalist philosophy—think grilled green asparagus with hazelnut butter, or crispy pommes soufflées served golden and light. Every detail is tuned to amplify the starring role of the steak. The wine list is serious without being intimidating, leaning into structured reds from Burgundy, Bordeaux and the Rhône alongside international selections chosen for their boldness and balance. Staff are knowledgeable, intuitive and fluent in guiding guests through both wine and beef choices with a quiet confidence that enhances the experience. In a city celebrated for its haute cuisine, Clover Grill is a place where meat is elevated to fine art. For those in search of flame, finesse and phenomenal steak in Paris, this address continues to impress—not with extravagance, but with purity, purpose and fire.

restaurants.name: GWEN
restaurants.notes: Curtis Stone’s Butcher-Driven Ode to Fire and Steak Dining in LA In the heart of Hollywood, Gwen by Chef Curtis Stone continues to redefine what a modern American steak restaurant can be. Equal parts European-style butcher shop, open-fire kitchen and refined dining room, Gwen delivers an experience that is as theatrical as it is deeply grounded in meat mastery. Since opening, Gwen has earned its place among America’s most respected meat-focused restaurants and in 2025, its influence only grows stronger. The space itself is dramatic and warm—polished brass, dark woods and visible dry-aging cabinets create a setting that is both opulent and raw, echoing the primal elegance of the fire-driven cuisine. At the heart of Gwen is Stone’s unwavering commitment to whole animal butchery and premium sourcing. Working closely with top ranchers and artisanal producers across the U.S., Australia and Tasmania. Gwen’s kitchen showcases heritage breeds, perfectly-aged cuts and rare finds, grilled over open flames with absolute precision. The tasting menu format, introduced for a more curated experience, leads guests through an evolving selection of the day’s best offerings—perhaps a dry-aged and grass-fed NY Strip from Cape Grim, Tasmania. Cuts are expertly seasoned, allowing the quality of the beef and the depth of smoke to shine without distraction. Supporting dishes are elegant and restrained—think charred carrots with harissa crème fraîche, duck fat potatoes, or a bone marrow custard that blurs the line between savoury and indulgent. The kitchen shows finesse beyond the grill, but never loses sight of its fire-forward core. The wine list is equally ambitious, with a cellar that balances Californian boldness and Old World structure. The sommelier team is highly knowledgeable, elevating each pairing with thoughtful intent. Service at Gwen is polished and attentive, yet unpretentious—a reflection of Stone’s ethos of refined hospitality without ego. The staff moves with confidence, clearly proud of the story they’re telling on every plate. Gwen remains a temple of meat in Los Angeles, where technique, sourcing and spectacle come together under the watchful eye of a chef-owner who treats every steak not just as a cut—but as a craft. For discerning carnivores and lovers of experiential dining alike, Gwen is essential.

restaurants.name: 4 CHARLES PRIME RIB
restaurants.notes: The kitchen is helmed by Executive Chef Tom Gannon, who brings a wealth of experience and a passion for precision to the establishment. Under his guidance, the culinary team delivers dishes that honour traditional steakhouse fare while incorporating contemporary nuances. Signature Dishes At the heart of the menu lies the eponymous prime rib, available in various cuts, including the indulgent “King’s Cut.” Each portion is expertly prepared to ensure a tender, flavourful experience that has garnered acclaim from both patrons and critics alike. Another standout is the double Wagyu cheeseburger.. This decadent offering features two juicy patties, melted cheese and a perfectly toasted bun, making it a must-try for burger enthusiasts. Atmosphere and Experience Securing a reservation at 4 Charles can be a challenge, given its limited seating and high demand. However, those fortunate enough to dine here are rewarded with impeccable service, a curated selection of fine wines and cocktails and an atmosphere that encourages leisurely, indulgent meals. 4 Charles Prime Rib stands out as a beacon for those seeking a refined steak restaurant experience that pays homage to tradition while embracing modern culinary excellence.

restaurants.name: GIMLET
restaurants.notes: European Elegance and Prime Steak, Melbourne-Style Housed in the grand Cavendish House on Russell Street, Gimlet continues to define sophisticated dining in Melbourne with timeless European charm and a distinct Australian edge. In 2025, under the steady hand of Chef Andrew McConnell, this CBD institution balances glamour and substance—now earning increasing recognition for its exceptional steak programme, which rivals the best in the country. While the allure of Gimlet’s velvet banquettes, mirrored walls and vintage chandeliers remains as strong as ever, what’s drawing a new wave of attention is what’s coming off the grill. The wood-fired hearth is the quiet hero of the kitchen, turning out deeply charred, perfectly rested steaks that speak of both precision and provenance. The steak selection is small but impeccable, with a focus on dry-aged, grass-fed beef from Victoria and South Australia. The O’Connor ribeye is a standout—intensely flavourful, generously marbled and cooked over flame to a perfect medium-rare with a smoky, caramelised crust. The porterhouse and rotating cuts from local heritage breeds round out a menu that places quality at its core. Beyond the meat, sides and sauces elevate the experience: potato galette, charred brassicas and a béarnaise that’s rich, glossy and expertly balanced. Every element feels deliberate—refined but deeply satisfying. Gimlet’s wine list remains one of the finest in Melbourne. It is both broad and deep, showcasing top Australian producers alongside storied European labels, with an emphasis on structure, texture and elegance—ideal companions to the complexity of the grill. Service is slick and well-paced. The front-of-house team glides with ease, confidently guiding guests through both wine and menu without intrusion. Their attentiveness and knowledge underscore Gimlet’s standing as not only a beautiful venue, but a deeply professional one. In 2025, Gimlet continues to dazzle with its trademark refinement, but it’s the rise of its steak offering that’s earning it a new level of reverence among Melbourne’s meat lovers.

restaurants.name: KITAN-IN
restaurants.notes: Kitan – Osaka’s Hidden Temple of Dry-Aged Wagyu Tucked away in the heart of Osaka’s Minami-Semba district, Kitan is a culinary sanctuary for beef connoisseurs. Helmed by Chef-owner Hiro-san, a Miyazaki native and seasoned wagyu artisan, Kitan offers an omakase-style yakiniku experience that celebrates the nuances of premium Japanese beef. Culinary Leadership Chef Hiro-san’s journey began in Miyazaki Prefecture, renowned for its exceptional wagyu. After six years of rigorous training, he ventured into the world of wagyu, establishing Kitan as a testament to his dedication and expertise. His philosophy centers on showcasing wagyu in its purest form, allowing the meat’s natural flavours to shine. Signature Dishes Kitan’s menu is a curated exploration of wagyu’s versatility: • Dry-Aged Wagyu Ribeye: Meticulously aged to enhance depth and tenderness, this cut exemplifies the restaurant’s commitment to quality. • Ichibo Tataki Steak: A delicate preparation highlighting the rich flavours of the ichibo cut, served with subtle accompaniments to elevate the experience. Each course is thoughtfully presented, reflecting Chef Hiro-san’s meticulous attention to detail and reverence for the ingredient. Ambience and Experience The restaurant’s intimate setting, featuring private rooms and a serene atmosphere, provides an ideal backdrop for the culinary journey. The minimalist design, infused with traditional Japanese elements, allows diners to focus on the flavours and textures presented. Kitan stands as a beacon for those seeking an authentic and refined wagyu experience in Osaka. Through Chef Hiro-san’s dedication and mastery, guests are treated to a dining experience that honors the rich traditions of Japanese beef preparation.

restaurants.name: KNIFE
restaurants.notes: In the heart of Dallas, Knife by Chef John Tesar has long stood as a bold reimagining of the American steakhouse—a place where dry-aged beef, culinary precision and Texan swagger converge to create one of the most compelling meat-centric experiences in Texas. Now a staple of the national dining scene and a fixture among the World’s Best Steak Restaurants, Knife in 2025 continues to push boundaries while celebrating the craft of butchery and the primal power of open flame. The space itself is sleek and modern, with an edge of urban grit—equal parts refined and rebellious, much like its chef. At the core of Knife’s identity is Tesar’s uncompromising meat programme. Steaks are aged in-house—some for up to 240 days—creating deep, nutty flavours and velvet-like textures rarely found outside of Europe. The 45-day dry-aged 44 Farms sirloin remains a house favourite, while rarities such as 240-day bone-in ribeyes and Outpost 76 beef speak to the restaurant’s position at the cutting edge of American beef culture. Tesar’s philosophy is rooted in transparency and respect for the animal. Each steak is presented with full traceability—breed, feed, age and method—reflecting a chef who treats meat not as a commodity, but as a story. Knife also dazzles beyond the grill. The bacon tasting, with five styles cured and smoked in-house, is a cult favourite, while the beef cheek ravioli, smoked short rib and bone marrow with oxtail marmalade reveal Tesar’s range and finesse. The wine list leans bold, with a particular focus on Napa Valley, Rhône varietals and expressive Spanish reds, each chosen to stand up to the restaurant’s richly marbled cuts. The cocktail programme, meanwhile, offers modern classics with an edge, ideal for a Dallas crowd that appreciates both power and playfulness. Service is sharp and engaging, with a team that reflects Tesar’s no-nonsense, high-integrity approach. They know their beef, respect their guests and keep the energy high without losing focus.

restaurants.name: ASADOR NICOLÁS
restaurants.notes: Basque Tradition and Open-Fire Mastery in Tolosa In the historic town of Tolosa, where fire and flesh have long defined the region’s most revered tables, Asador Nicolás stands proudly among the very best. This is Basque grilling at its purest, where simplicity, quality and mastery over flame come together to produce one of Spain’s most memorable steak experiences. The heart of Asador Nicolás is, without question, the chuleta—typically from mature, well-aged dairy cows, cooked over an open wood fire to blistered, smoky perfection. In 2025, this dish remains the soul of the restaurant: seared with high heat to form a crisp, caramelised crust and served rare to reveal the beef’s deep, earthy richness and bold, mineral complexity. It’s not just grilled meat—it’s a celebration of animal, time and flame. Chef-owner Xabi Ruiz and his team honour every cut with absolute respect, using traditional techniques passed down through generations of Basque asadores. The chuleta is carved tableside with quiet confidence and always served simply—with a dusting of salt, a side of piquillo peppers and perhaps a few crisp fried potatoes. No theatrics, no unnecessary flourish—just elemental perfection. The ambience is rustic, warm and grounded. Wood beams, stone walls and the glow of the grill remind you this is a place of heritage, not trends. Locals fill the tables as frequently as culinary pilgrims and the rhythm of the meal feels communal, generous and grounded in deep tradition. The wine list is rooted in the region, offering structured Rioja, Ribera del Duero and some bold international selections, all chosen to complement the power and depth of the chuleta. Staff are attentive and proud of what they serve, moving with a quiet rhythm that mirrors the kitchen’s steady pace. Also in 2025, Asador Nicolás remains a benchmark of authentic Basque grilling, where the fire is not just a cooking method, but a language. For steak purists and those in search of the truest expression of chuleta culture, this Tolosa institution is not just worth the journey—it is the destination. .

restaurants.name: NIKU STEAKHOUSE
restaurants.notes: Niku Steakhouse – Fire and Precision in the Heart of San Francisco Tucked into the vibrant Design District of San Francisco, Niku Steakhouse continues to define what modern, Japanese-influenced steak restaurant dining can be. In 2025, it remains a destination for those seeking refined fire-cooked meats, artisan technique and a distinct East-meets-West aesthetic—guided by the precise and forward-thinking leadership of Executive Chef Dustin Falcon. The space itself is stylish and immersive, combining industrial sophistication with warm, elemental textures. At its core stands an open binchōtan-fuelled robata grill, a glowing symbol of the restaurant’s devotion to smoke, heat and minimalist purity. At Niku, beef is not just served—it is revered. The in-house whole animal butchery programme allows for a meticulously curated steak offering that includes American-raised Japanese Wagyu and locally sourced, dry-aged cuts. Each steak is grilled with utmost restraint, allowing the natural marbling and depth of the meat to take centre stage. Chef Falcon’s menu reflects Japanese sensibility and Californian ingenuity. Beyond the beef, dishes such as mt. lassen trout or the bone marrow-topped tartare add sophistication and dimension to the experience. Each course is built with clarity, balance and a quiet confidence in the product. The wine and cocktail programme is equally compelling. The wine list offers depth across Burgundy, Napa and small international producers, with a strong sommelier team guiding guests with intuition and precision. The cocktail list leans toward Japanese botanicals, barrel-aged spirits and clean, umami-forward profiles—each drink designed to accompany the fire-forward cuisine. Service at Niku is polished and graceful. From the welcome to the final pour, the team moves with calm authority, deeply informed about both the meats and the techniques used to elevate them. The attention to detail is evident in every gesture and pairing suggestion. In 2025, Niku Steakhouse remains a powerful expression where the quality of the meat is matched only by the technique and care with which it’s handled. Under the vision of Chef Dustin Falcon, it stands as one of San Francisco’s most confident dining experiences.

restaurants.name: FIRESIDE
restaurants.street: 5th floor, H Code The Steps, 45 Pottinger St
restaurants.notes: Fireside – Primitive Elegance and Open Flame Mastery in Hong Kong In the bustling centre of Hong Kong’s Central district, Fireside has quietly become one of the city’s most compelling meat-centric dining destinations. Led by Head Chef Jaime Ortolá, this intimate and fire-focused restaurant celebrates primitive cooking methods through a lens of modern sophistication—where smoke, embers and raw product take precedence over unnecessary technique. At Fireside, the centrepiece isn’t just the food—it’s the flame. Every element of the menu passes through fire, whether it’s kissed by coals, smoked gently over wood, or blistered in custom-built charcoal and wood-fire ovens. The open kitchen invites diners to witness this elemental process firsthand, bridging the gap between chef and guest in a way that feels immersive and personal. Chef Ortolá’s culinary vision revolves around ingredient purity and honest technique. The menu changes frequently, depending on seasonal availability and sourcing, but always reflects a deep respect for product. Highlights in 2025 include Galician beef ribeye aged over 100 days, whole grilled carabinero prawns and a showstopping Iberico pork rack—each prepared with restraint and precision, allowing smoke and fat to do the storytelling. Small plates such as wood-roasted bone marrow with citrus gremolata, coal-grilled vegetables and open-flame sourdough with smoked butter offer thoughtful interludes between heavier courses. The style is unpretentious yet deeply technical—flavour-forward, primal and elegant all at once. The wine programme is a highlight in its own right, showcasing a smartly curated list of Old World favourites and low-intervention finds that complement the depth and umami of the fire-cooked dishes. The staff’s pairing suggestions reflect an intimate knowledge of the kitchen’s rhythm and the evolving menu. Service at Fireside is warm and intuitive, mirroring the atmosphere of the glowing hearth. The team moves with grace, offering insights into sourcing, fire technique and ingredient provenance without ever overexplaining. Fireside stands as one of Hong Kong’s most exciting restaurants - not because of spectacle or scale, but because of how masterfully it strips things back to the flame, the meat and the moment. Under Chef Jaime Ortolá’s thoughtful guidance, it’s a destination for those who crave authenticity, warmth and the timeless magic of open-fire cooking.

restaurants.name: SHELL HOUSE
restaurants.notes: Shell House Dining Room & Terrace – A Sky-High Celebration of Meat in Sydney Perched atop a heritage-listed building in Sydney’s CBD, Shell House Dining Room & Terrace offers a refined yet approachable dining experience. Under the guidance of Culinary Director Joel Bickford and Head Chef Brad Guest, the menu showcases premium Australian meats prepared with precision and flair. Meat-Centric Highlights • 600g Fiorentina Steak: A Black Angus MB2+ T-bone, grilled to perfection and served with rocket and fries, epitomizing the restaurant’s commitment to quality cuts. • Bistecca al Pepe: Flank steak accompanied by a robust pepper sauce, offering a classic yet elevated flavour profile. • Wagyu Beef Burger: A succulent patty paired with cheese and pickles, served alongside crispy fries, delivering comfort with a gourmet twist. These dishes highlight Shell House’s dedication to sourcing exceptional meats and preparing them with techniques that enhance their natural flavours. Ambience and Service The dining room exudes elegance with its blend of classic and contemporary design elements, complemented by the L-shaped garden terrace that offers panoramic city views. Service is attentive and knowledgeable, ensuring a dining experience that is both comfortable and memorable. Conclusion Shell House Dining Room & Terrace stands as a testament to Sydney’s evolving culinary scene, offering meat enthusiasts a destination where quality cuts and expert preparation converge in a stunning setting.

restaurants.name: JEFFREY’S
restaurants.notes: Jeffrey’s - Austin’s Iconic Intersection of Fine Dining and Steakhouse Tradition A beacon of elegance in the heart of Clarksville, Jeffrey’s has long been one of Austin’s most celebrated dining destinations. In 2025, it continues to impress with its seamless blend of classic fine dining atmosphere, southern charm and a steak programme that ranks among the finest in the country. Housed in a restored 1930s cottage, Jeffrey’s feels timeless. Dark wood, plush velvet banquettes and low, golden lighting set the tone for a meal that feels at once intimate and grand. The service is polished and warm—effortlessly guiding guests through a menu that champions American beef excellence with French-influenced finesse. At the heart of the menu are the steaks, which are aged in-house and cooked to perfection over a custom wood-fired grill. The cuts are thoughtfully sourced, with offerings like the Niman Ranch bone-in ribeye, Mishima Reserve New York strip and the showstopping filet mignon wrapped in bacon and finished with truffle butter. Each steak is served simply, letting the quality of the meat—and the skill in its preparation—speak volumes. Beyond the grill, Jeffrey’s excels in refinement. Starters like truffled devilled eggs, pork belly rilletes and the braised beef short rib’s offer richness and balance. Sides such as duck fat potatoes, crispy Brussels sprouts and creamed spinach with nutmeg and garlic confit provide classic pairings elevated by meticulous execution. The wine list is among the most celebrated in Austin, with deep verticals of Napa Cabernet, Burgundy and Bordeaux, as well as rare Old World treasures and emerging producers. The sommelier team is gracious and intuitive, offering expert pairings that enhance every bite. Service at Jeffrey’s remains a hallmark of the experience—attentive but never intrusive, with every detail handled with care, from the table-side steak presentations to the final pour of dessert wine. For us Jeffrey’s remains the gold standard for elevated steak restaurant dining in Austin and beyond - a place where tradition, technique and genuine hospitality converge. For those seeking the perfect steak in an atmosphere of quiet luxury, Jeffrey’s continues to lead with confidence and class.

restaurants.name: BISTECCA TUSCAN STEAKHOUSE
restaurants.notes: Tuscan Soul and Wood-Fired Perfection in the Heart of Singapore Tucked away on Mohamed Sultan Road, Bistecca Tuscan Steakhouse continues to assert itself in 2025 as one of Singapore’s most revered destinations for steak lovers. With its inviting blend of Italian rustic charm and big-city polish, Bistecca delivers a steakhouse experience that is equal parts soulful, luxurious and unapologetically carnivorous. The restaurant’s interior oozes warmth—exposed brick walls, candlelit tables, dark woods and low lighting create a sense of timeless intimacy that feels like stepping into a Florentine trattoria transported to Southeast Asia. It’s a space that encourages lingering, storytelling and most of all, indulgence. At the heart of Bistecca is its signature Bistecca alla Fiorentina - a 1.1kg bone-in, thick-cut T-bone steak made with purebred F1 Wagyu from Australia, raised specifically for the restaurant and dry-aged in-house. Cooked over a wood-fired grill to produce a crisp char and a succulent, rare centre, the steak is a triumph of both flavour and texture: rich, juicy and deeply aromatic. Other standout cuts include the Bone-in Ribeye, Tenderloin and rotating specials that often feature heritage or grass-fed breeds—all grilled over fire with precision and paired with Tuscan salt and olive oil. Every steak here is about balance—fat and smoke, richness and restraint—executed with an understanding of both Tuscan tradition and modern technique. The supporting menu is equally thoughtful. Antipasti like Burrata with eggplant caponata, charred octopus, or hand-cut steak tartare set the tone, while classic contorni—from creamy spinach to Tuscan fries with rosemary and sea salt—round out the experience with rustic charm. The wine list is steeped in Italian pride, with verticals from Chianti, Barolo and Brunello sitting alongside carefully chosen New World reds. The sommelier team is passionate and approachable, offering intuitive pairings that enhance the fire-kissed cuts and regional accompaniments. Service at Bistecca reflects the Italian philosophy of genuine hospitality—relaxed yet refined, attentive but never overbearing. Staff move with a grace that makes every guest feel like they’re dining in a friend’s elegant home. Also in 2025, Bistecca Tuscan Steakhouse remains one of Singapore’s leading destinations for serious steak, where Italian heritage, premium Wagyu and wood-fired precision come together in spectacular harmony. For those seeking flavour, fire and feeling, Bistecca delivers - every single time.

restaurants.name: KOKI
restaurants.notes: A Japanese Culinary Sanctuary at Capella Hanoi Tucked beneath the opulent Capella Hanoi in Vietnam’s historic French Quarter, Koki offers an immersive journey into Japanese gastronomy. Led by Head Chef Yamaguchi Hiroshi, the restaurant seamlessly blends traditional techniques with contemporary flair, delivering an experience that engages all five senses. Culinary Leadership Chef Yamaguchi Hiroshi, brings decades of expertise to Koki. His philosophy centres on the harmonious union of umami (the fifth taste), kansha (deep respect for ingredients) and omotenashi (the art of Japanese hospitality). Each dish he crafts embodies a flawless equilibrium of flavours, showcasing his profound mastery of the culinary arts. Signature Dishes Koki’s menu is a testament to the purity and precision of Japanese cuisine. Highlights include: • Yaeyama Kyori Beef: Sourced exclusively from Okinawa’s Ishigaki Island, this premium beef is celebrated for its rich marbling and depth of flavour. • Seasonal Omakase: A curated tasting menu that evolves with the seasons, showcasing the freshest ingredients and Chef Yamaguchi’s innovative techniques. Ambience and Experience Koki’s interior exudes understated elegance, with minimalist design elements that reflect the Japanese aesthetic of kanso (simplicity). The intimate setting allows guests to witness the culinary artistry firsthand, enhancing the dining experience. Koki at Capella Hanoi stands as a beacon of Japanese culinary excellence in Vietnam. Through Chef Yamaguchi’s dedication and skill, the restaurant offers an unparalleled dining experience that celebrates the rich traditions and flavours of Japan.

restaurants.name: BLOK
restaurants.street: Lanelay Hall Hotel, Lanelay Lane, Talbot Green
restaurants.notes: Fine Dining by Fire in the Heart of South Wales Nestled within the elegant Lanelay Hall Hotel & Spa in Pontyclun, BLOK offers a refined dining experience that celebrates the art of cooking over fire. Under the guidance of Head Chef James Milward, the restaurant presents a menu that highlights premium meats and locally sourced produce, all prepared with meticulous attention to detail. Culinary Leadership Chef James Milward brings a wealth of experience to BLOK, having previously honed his skills at esteemed establishments in the region. His expertise ensures that each dish at BLOK is crafted with precision and creativity. The menu at BLOK features a selection of expertly prepared meats cooked over open fire, including: • Dry Aged Sirloin, Rib-Eye or Tomahawk on the bone These cuts are complemented by a variety of great sauces, such as Café de Paris butter, brown butter béarnaise and green mojo, enhancing the natural flavours of the meats. BLOK’s interior design combines rich textures and a warm, natural palette to create an inviting atmosphere. The kitchen concept allows diners to witness the culinary craftsmanship firsthand, adding a theatrical element to the dining experience. BLOK stands as a testament to the harmonious blend of traditional cooking methods and contemporary culinary artistry. For those seeking a memorable dining experience in South Wales, BLOK offers a journey through flavours that are both comforting and innovative.

restaurants.name: ELENA
restaurants.notes: Situated within the grand Four Seasons Hotel Buenos Aires, Elena continues to stand as one of South America’s most celebrated dining destinations in 2025. Under the expert direction of Executive Chef Juan Gaffuri, Elena offers a seamless blend of Argentine grilling tradition and international culinary sophistication, earning its place among the world’s elite steak experiences. The restaurant’s atmosphere strikes the perfect balance between luxury and warmth. With its two-storey layout, exposed brick, leather seating, iron detailing and a signature dry-ageing cabinet on full display, Elena evokes the feel of a European brasserie grounded in Argentine soul—a space made for indulgence, conversation and unforgettable meals. At the heart of the experience is an exceptional meat programme, featuring the finest Argentinian Black Angus, local Wagyu and dry-aged cuts matured in-house. Each steak is expertly grilled over open flame to enhance flavour, texture and aroma. Simplicity is key, allowing the quality of the beef to shine through with just a touch of salt and fire. Chef Gaffuri’s menu is a tribute to craftsmanship and terroir. Beyond the grill, highlights include hand-cut beef tartare with yolk cream, house-cured charcuterie and smoked vegetables with Argentine olive oil and ash salt—each dish reflecting a refined understanding of regional flavour. The wine programme is proudly rooted in Argentina, showcasing the best from Mendoza, Patagonia and the Calchaquí Valleys, alongside iconic Old World selections. The sommelier team offers thoughtful, tailored pairings, enhancing the experience without overshadowing the food. Service at Elena is impeccable—professional, warm and instinctive, embodying the Four Seasons’ dedication to hospitality while retaining a deeply local sensibility. In 2025, Elena is not only a benchmark for steak in Buenos Aires but a global reference for what happens when fire, tradition and culinary precision meet at the highest level. With Chef Juan Gaffuri at the helm, it remains a must-visit for lovers of bold, honest and world-class cuisine.

restaurants.name: MATILDA 159 DOMAIN
restaurants.notes: Matilda 159 Domain, situated in Melbourne’s South Yarra, is a culinary haven where the primal allure of open flames meets refined Australian dining. Conceived by acclaimed chef Scott Pickett, this establishment pays homage to his South Australian roots and is named after his daughter, Matilda. Here, the art of cooking over fire is not just a method but a philosophy, delivering dishes that are both robust and nuanced. The menu at Matilda is a testament to Australia’s rich produce and diverse flavours. The interior design, crafted by Projects of Imagination, mirrors the restaurant’s culinary ethos. With tables made from Otway Blackwood and botanical installations that change with the seasons, the space exudes a warm, earthy elegance. The open kitchen, equipped with a Josper oven, rotisserie and wood-fired grill, allows guests to witness the theatricality of fire-based cooking firsthand. Complementing the food is an extensive beverage list featuring a curated selection of local and international wines, as well as craft beers and spirits. The knowledgeable staff are adept at guiding diners through pairings that enhance the flavours of each dish. Matilda 159 Domain is more than a restaurant; it’s an immersive experience that captures the essence of Australian cuisine through the elemental force of fire. Under Scott Pickett’s visionary leadership, it stands as a beacon of culinary excellence in Melbourne’s vibrant dining scene.

restaurants.name: KEENS
restaurants.notes: A New Chapter for a New York Legend In a city built on institutions, Keens Steakhouse has always stood apart—a relic of old Manhattan, rich with history, character and the unmistakable aroma of perfectly broiled beef. Founded in 1885 and revered for its mutton chop, pipe-lined ceilings and clubby charm, Keens is more than just a restaurant—it’s a time capsule of New York dining at its most iconic. In 2025, however, Keens enters a new era, following a recent change in ownership. While the walls remain lined with clay pipes and the air still carries the scent of dry-aged meat and legacy, there is a quiet shift underway. With the transition still fresh, longtime patrons and newcomers alike are watching with curiosity to see how this beloved institution will evolve under new stewardship. At the heart of the experience, Keens continues to deliver bold, unapologetically classic cuts, with its legendary Prime Rib, Porterhouse and the famed Mutton Chop (a beautifully marbled saddle of lamb) still taking centre stage. The steaks are USDA Prime, expertly broiled and served simply—unchanged, unpretentious and unforgettable. For now, the menu retains its signature offerings, a nod to the weight of tradition that surrounds every plate. The space remains much as it always has: dark wood, white tablecloths and walls that whisper stories of playwrights, presidents and Wall Street titans. It’s a room that breathes with the soul of New York. The wine list is extensive and well-anchored in robust reds—Cabernets, Bordeaux and old-school Napa—all chosen to match the richness of the meat. Service, still seasoned and sharply professional, adds to the comforting rhythm of a place that knows exactly what it is. Yet with the change in ownership, the future of Keens is quietly in motion. Will the new proprietors preserve its storied legacy or steer it toward a more modern identity? Only time will tell. For now, the essence of Keens remains intact and guests continue to return for the flavour, the nostalgia and the sense that some things—at least for the moment—are just as they always were. Keens stands not only as a steakhouse but as a cultural monument. The steaks are still excellent. The room is still magic. But all eyes are on what comes next.

restaurants.name: CUT BY WOLFGANG PUCK
restaurants.notes: CUT by Wolfgang Puck – Singapore’s Modern Steakhouse Excellence Nestled within the opulent surroundings of Marina Bay Sands, CUT by Wolfgang Puck stands as one of Singapore’s premier destination for steak aficionados. At the helm is Executive Chef Greg Bess, a seasoned professional who has been instrumental in shaping CUT’s esteemed reputation since its inception. A graduate of Le Cordon Bleu in Pasadena, California, Chef Bess honed his skills at the original Spago in Beverly Hills before bringing his expertise to Singapore in 2010 . His leadership ensures that each dish reflects both innovation and a deep respect for tradition. Signature Dishes CUT’s menu is a curated selection of the finest cuts from around the globe, including: • Hokkaido Snow Beef: Renowned for its exceptional marbling and tenderness. • Kagoshima Wagyu: A Japanese delicacy celebrated for its rich flavour profile. • Snake River Farms American “Kobe Style” Beef: A premium selection that offers a harmonious balance of texture and taste. Each steak is meticulously seasoned and grilled to perfection, ensuring a dining experience that delights the palate. Grilling Technique The culinary team employs a distinctive grilling method that involves searing steaks over aromatic charcoal and apple wood, followed by a precise char under a 1,200-degree broiler . This technique imparts a unique depth of flavour and a perfectly caramelised crust to each cut, setting CUT apart in the realm of steakhouse dining. The restaurant’s interior exudes modern elegance, featuring a sleek design that complements its culinary offerings. Guests can expect an atmosphere that is both inviting and refined, making it an ideal setting for intimate dinners and special occasions alike. CUT by Wolfgang Puck in Singapore offers great steak restaurant dining, combining world-class ingre Central to The Cut’s culinary philosophy is its commitment to traditional wood-fired grilling. Utilising a custom-built wood-fired grill, the kitchen imparts a distinctive smoky flavour to its dishes, enhancing the natural qualities of the premium meats and seafood. This method ensures a perfect sear and depth of flavour that is both authentic and refined. The restaurant’s interior exudes understated elegance, featuring exposed brick walls, timber beams and ambient lighting that create a warm and inviting atmosphere. Whether seated in a plush leather booth or at the polished bar, guests are enveloped in an environment that balances comfort with sophistication. The Cut Bar & Grill stands as a testament to Sydney’s vibrant dining scene, offering a harmonious blend of tradition and innovation. With its expert culinary team, commitment to quality and inviting ambience, it provides an unparalleled steakhouse experience in the heart of the city.

restaurants.name: THE BAZAAR
restaurants.street: The Ritz Carlton New York, Nomad, 25 W 28th Street
restaurants.notes: The Bazaar NYC by José Andrés Nestled within the Ritz-Carlton New York, Nomad - The Bazaar by José Andrés offers a theatrical homage to meat in its most indulgent forms. While the restaurant’s playful small plates and molecular gastronomy nods are noteworthy, it’s the array of steak cuts that truly steal the spotlight. Beyond traditional cuts, The Bazaar introduces inventive dishes that highlight beef’s versatility: • Beef Tartare: A deconstructed presentation featuring Japanese Wagyu top sirloin, egg yolk, mustard and anchovies, assembled tableside and served with tempura shiso leaves. • Wagyu Air Bread: A signature item where airy bread is filled with melted manchego cheese and topped with thinly sliced, lightly seared Wagyu beef, offering a delightful contrast of textures. The restaurant’s ambiance complements its menu - opulent yet inviting, with an open kitchen that allows diners to witness the meticulous preparation of each dish. The knowledgeable staff enhances the experience, guiding guests through the menu and suggesting pairings from an extensive wine and cocktail list. The Bazaar by José Andrés offers a unique dining adventure, especially for those interested in avant-garde cuisine and high-quality meats. However, potential patrons should be prepared for premium pricing and a dining experience that emphasizes presentation and innovation over traditional portion sizes. If you’re seeking a culinary journey that combines artistry with flavour, The Bazaar provides an experience that’s both memorable and distinctive.

restaurants.name: CHAR FUEGO Y BRASAS
restaurants.street: Baobab Suites, C. Roques del Salmor, 6
restaurants.notes: Culinary Artistry Over Fire Chef Fall, often referred to as a true artist of fire, brings a unique blend of passion and precision to the grill. His mastery is evident in the meticulous preparation of premium meats sourced from renowned Spanish regions such as Galicia, the Basque Country and Teruel. Notably, the restaurant features Luismi beef, celebrated for its exceptional quality and flavour. The culinary process is a harmonious dance between traditional techniques and innovative flair. Chef Fall employs a combination of Basque-style grills, charcoal ovens and cast-iron pans to achieve the perfect sear and succulence in each cut. This triad of cooking methods ensures a depth of flavour that is both robust and refined. Signature Dishes • Steak Tartare “Char”: A finely chopped delicacy made from Luismi beef, seasoned with a blend of spices and served with pickled vegetables and marrow emulsion. • Grilled Octopus: Tender octopus grilled to perfection, showcasing the restaurant’s expertise beyond red meats. • Char Fire Menu: A curated tasting experience that takes diners on a journey through the restaurant’s finest offerings, highlighting the versatility and depth of flavours achievable through fire-based cooking. Ambience and Experience The interior of Char Fuego y Brasas exudes a warm and inviting atmosphere, with an open kitchen concept that allows guests to witness the culinary artistry firsthand. The attentive and knowledgeable staff further enhance the dining experience, providing insights into the menu and ensuring each visit is memorable. Conclusion Char Fuego y Brasas stands as a testament to the transformative power of fire in culinary arts. Through Chef Babacar Fall’s dedication and skill, the restaurant offers an unparalleled dining experience that celebrates the rich flavours and traditions of Spanish grilling. For those seeking a gastronomic meat centric experience in Spain, Char Fuego y Brasas is an essential destination.

restaurants.name: THE CUT BAR & GRILL
restaurants.notes: Nestled within the historic sandstone walls of The Rocks, The Cut Bar & Grill offers a sophisticated yet rustic dining experience that pays homage to classic New York steakhouses. Following a meticulous renovation, the restaurant has re-emerged as a beacon for meat enthusiasts, seamlessly blending heritage charm with contemporary culinary artistry. At the helm of the kitchen is Chef de Cuisine Krishan Raju, whose culinary expertise ensures that each dish resonates with flavour and precision . Collaborating closely with Executive Chef Santiago Aristizabal, known for his tenure at Rockpool Bar & Grill, the duo curates a menu that celebrates premium cuts and innovative preparations . The Cut’s menu is a testament to the art of grilling, featuring an array of dishes that highlight the depth and versatility of wood-fired cooking: • Slow-Roasted Prime Rib: A house specialty, this succulent cut is meticulously slow-cooked and carved tableside, offering a theatrical and flavourful experience . • David Blackmore Wagyu Rump Cap (9+): Sourced from renowned Australian producer David Blackmore, this wagyu cut boasts exceptional marbling and tenderness. • Rangers Valley Black Onyx Hanger Steak: Known for its rich flavour, this hanger steak is a favourite among discerning diners. Complementing the meats are thoughtfully crafted sides such as creamed spinach with Gruyère cheese and crispy Royal Blue potatoes with barbecued onion and sage. Central to The Cut’s culinary philosophy is its commitment to traditional wood-fired grilling. Utilising a custom-built wood-fired grill, the kitchen imparts a distinctive smoky flavour to its dishes, enhancing the natural qualities of the premium meats and seafood. This method ensures a perfect sear and depth of flavour that is both authentic and refined. The restaurant’s interior exudes understated elegance, featuring exposed brick walls, timber beams and ambient lighting that create a warm and inviting atmosphere. Whether seated in a plush leather booth or at the polished bar, guests are enveloped in an environment that balances comfort with sophistication. The Cut Bar & Grill stands as a testament to Sydney’s vibrant dining scene, offering a harmonious blend of tradition and innovation. With its expert culinary team, commitment to quality and inviting ambience, it provides an unparalleled steakhouse experience in the heart of the city.

restaurants.name: MINETTA TAVERN
restaurants.notes: Timeless Glamour and Steakhouse Grandeur in the Heart of Greenwich Village Few restaurants in New York wear their history as effortlessly as Minetta Tavern. Tucked away on MacDougal Street in Greenwich Village, this legendary dining room remains, in 2025, one of Manhattan’s most enduring icons—a place where Parisian bistro soul meets New York steakhouse swagger and the ghosts of literary giants still seem to linger in the corners. Reimagined years ago by restaurateur Keith McNally, Minetta Tavern retains its romantic patina: red leather banquettes, black-and-white portraits, soft globe lighting and a sense that something special is always happening just out of view. The atmosphere is at once intimate and electric—buzzing with old-school New York charm and ageless appeal. At the core of Minetta’s fame is the now-iconic Black Label Burger, a dry-aged blend of prime cuts that remains one of the most sought-after bites in the city. But beyond the burger, Minetta has quietly built one of New York’s great meat programmes—anchored by the dry-aged côte de boeuf, a richly marbled steak meant for sharing and grilled to perfection. Other standout offerings include bone-in New York strip, roasted bone marrow and butcher’s cuts aged in-house, each dish a reminder that Minetta is not simply about nostalgia—it is about craft. Appetisers like steak tartare, foie gras terrine and warm poached bone marrow with shallot marmalade strike a luxurious balance between French technique and New York boldness. The sides—pommes frites, creamed spinach, or sautéed wild mushrooms—are classic and satisfying, executed with the same attention to detail. The wine list is thoughtful and quietly confident, favouring Bordeaux, Burgundy and Napa Cabs, but with room for boutique French and Italian producers. The cocktail programme, meanwhile, celebrates pre-war classics with a twist—perfectly suited to the room’s smoky, literary glamour. Service at Minetta Tavern is as it should be—professional, gracious and timed to the rhythm of the room. Whether you’re a regular or a first-time guest, you’re treated like someone who belongs. In 2025, Minetta Tavern remains a love letter to New York’s golden age of dining and one of the city’s most atmospheric spots to enjoy a world-class steak. It is not trying to be new and that is precisely its power. For those who seek history, theatre and perfectly charred beef, Minetta is not just relevant—it’s eternal.

restaurants.name: GRILL ROYAL
restaurants.notes: If there is one address in Germany where steak meets spectacle, it’s Grill Royal. Perched along the Spree river with floor-to-ceiling views and a nightly procession of artists, politicians and global tastemakers, this restaurant has, in 2025, solidified its status as the capital’s definitive dining room for meat, mood and momentum. The ambiance is unmistakably Berlin—eclectic, glamorous and effortlessly cool. The dining room, adorned with contemporary artworks, low-slung lighting and a view that stretches across the city’s glittering heart, sets the stage for an experience that is equal parts culinary and cultural. It’s a place where grilled ribeye and raw conversations share equal billing. At the heart of Grill Royal is its robust meat programme, with a menu that reads like a global atlas of prime cuts. From German Simmental and Irish Hereford to Japanese Wagyu and U.S. Prime, each steak is meticulously sourced, dry-aged where appropriate and grilled over high heat to crusted, juicy perfection. The Chateaubriand, the dry-aged ribeye and the bavette with bone marrow butter are perennial favourites - showcasing both technical skill and ingredient purity. But Grill Royal isn’t only about steak - it’s about luxury without stiffness. The kitchen excels in seafood and vegetables too, with options like grilled wild turbot, hand-cut beef tartare and truffle-dressed greens offering elegant counterpoints to the richness of the grill. The wine list is deep and worldly, leaning heavily on Bordeaux, Barolo and Burgundy, with a strong showing of German Spätburgunder and Riesling. There’s also a bold cocktail menu and an unspoken house rule that champagne never needs a reason. Service is sharp and unflustered, with a team that navigates the full house of personalities - from VIPs to artists to first-timers with style, discretion and just enough Berlin edge. We think also in 2025, Grill Royal remains Berlin’s power table - a place where influence is as much on the plate as in the room. It’s a restaurant that understands that great steak is about more than meat, it’s about atmosphere, timing and presence. For those who want to dine where flame meets finesse and glamour never feels forced, Grill Royal is still the place to be.

restaurants.name: BEEF & GLORY
restaurants.notes: beef & glory – Vienna’s Carnivorous Landmark at a Crossroads Once a bold newcomer that helped redefine Vienna’s steakhouse culture, beef & glory remains in 2025 a well-known name for meat lovers in the Austrian capital. Located just a short stroll from the MuseumsQuartier, the restaurant built its reputation on premium cuts, dramatic dry-aging and a no-compromise approach to grilled beef—a philosophy that, at its peak, positioned it among Europe’s rising steak destinations. The interior still carries that urban-chic charm: dark wood, industrial accents, all styled to evoke modern steakhouse cool. The wine wall remains well stocked, the lights low and the energy high. It’s a place that still draws in a devoted local following—and rightly so. The menu offers a broad range of dry-aged and grain-fed cuts, sourced primarily from Austria, the US and Japan. Standout selections such as the “Alte Kuh X.O.” beef, Miyazaki Wagyu strip, or the house-aged porterhouse remain solid, both in flavour and execution. When the kitchen is focused, the grill work still delivers: beautiful sear, proper rest, deep flavour. However, in 2025, beef & glory finds itself at a moment of reflection. In an increasingly competitive international scene, the concept now feels slightly overextended—with a menu that’s wide rather than sharp and a style that sometimes seems to chase trend rather than set it. The result is a dining experience that, while still satisfying, lacks the clarity and intensity that once defined it. The service remains warm and confident and the team clearly cares. But the soul of the restaurant—once rooted in purity, fire and exceptional sourcing—would benefit from returning to the essentials. A more focused offering, stripped of distraction, would allow the quality of the beef and the passion of the team to shine through once again. The wine list continues to impress, particularly for fans of bold Austrian reds and international cabernets, with thoughtful pairings available for every cut. Today beef & glory is still the respected meat-centric player in Austria’s dining scene. But if it is to reclaim its former edge in the global steakhouse conversation, it must rediscover its core: fire, flavour and simplicity. Less is more - and meat deserves nothing less.

restaurants.name: SHATŌBURIAN
restaurants.notes: Shatoburian, nestled within Singapore’s Palais Renaissance, stands as a sanctuary for wagyu aficionados seeking an unparalleled dining experience. Specialising in premium Japanese A5 wagyu, the restaurant offers a meticulously curated menu that celebrates the rich marbling and delicate flavours characteristic of this esteemed beef. The establishment sources its wagyu exclusively from authorised Japanese auction houses, ensuring each cut meets the highest standards of quality. From the tender chateaubriand to the flavourful misuji, every selection is prepared with precision, allowing the natural essence of the meat to shine. Signature offerings include the Shato-Sando, featuring chateaubriand wagyu paired with glazed foie gras and freshly grated truffle and the Premium Wagyu Don, a luxurious bowl combining steak-cut wagyu, unagi, foie gras, sea urchin, ikura and onsen egg, all topped with shaved truffles. The dining experience is further enhanced by attentive service and an elegant ambiance, making Shatoburian a distinguished destination for those seeking to indulge in the finest expressions of wagyu cuisine.

restaurants.name: HOLSTEINS
restaurants.notes: Holsteins - Mexico’s Temple of Fire and Breed-Driven Steak Excellence Set against the dynamic backdrop of Monterrey’s bustling culinary scene, Holsteins has, by 2025, firmly established itself as one of Mexico’s most ambitious and serious steak destinations. With a philosophy deeply rooted in breed, origin and fire, Holsteins has become a must-visit for meat aficionados who seek more than just a grilled cut—they seek a story on the plate. The space is bold yet warm—modern Mexican minimalism meets ranch elegance, with stone, leather and iron accents framing an open kitchen where fire is not just a cooking method, but a performance. The atmosphere is refined, but never cold; it’s a restaurant that wears its confidence lightly. Holsteins’ reputation has been built on its uncompromising approach to beef, particularly its work with purebred and crossbred Holstein cattle, as well as Angus and Wagyu varieties, many of which are raised and dry-aged through direct partnerships with local and regional producers. The menu showcases this depth with cuts such as the Holstein Prime bone-in ribeye, the dry-aged sirloin and a signature Australian Wagyu striploin—each grilled over open flame with precision and reverence. But the restaurant doesn’t stop at technique. The menu is educational, not performative—highlighting breed, feed, ageing duration and origin for each cut, allowing guests to connect with the meat before it ever hits the grill. Supporting dishes are equally strong: beef fat tortillas, grilled tuétano (bone marrow) with smoked chile ash and sides of native corn purée or caramelised onions with mezcal glaze—all nods to northern Mexico’s rich culinary heritage, interpreted with modern finesse. The wine and agave programme is one of the finest in the region. From structured reds from Valle de Guadalupe to elegant expressions of mezcal and sotol, the beverage pairings are thoughtfully curated to balance the intensity of the fire and fat. Service is confident, professional and personal, with a team helmed by Restaurant Manager Claudio Rivero that understands the rhythm of a serious steakhouse, but delivers it with Mexican warmth and hospitality. In 2025, Holsteins is not just a steakhouse—it is a statement. A statement about craft, region and identity. With its deep respect for provenance and breed and a kitchen that understands the power of simplicity over fire, Holsteins is leading the charge for a new generation of Mexican meat culture—one that is local, world-class and defiantly proud.

restaurants.name: FIREWOOD
restaurants.notes: Firewood, nestled in the heart of George Town, Penang, is a culinary haven where the primal allure of open flames meets refined Malaysian dining. Founded by chefs Ker Yang Hao and Lew Jia Jiun, alumni of Gordon Ramsay in Singapore, the restaurant pays homage to their expertise in flame-centric cooking. Here, the art of cooking over fire is not just a method but a philosophy, delivering dishes that are both robust and nuanced. The menu at Firewood is a testament to Malaysia’s rich produce and diverse flavours. Signature dishes include the grain-fed Australian Angus sirloin, grilled to perfection and complemented by a vibrant chimichurri sauce and the Japan A5 Wagyu Miyazaki sirloin, known for its marbling and tenderness. Each plate is a harmonious blend of tradition and innovation, showcasing ingredients like locally sourced seafood and seasonal vegetables. The chefs’ commitment to quality is evident in their meticulous preparation and presentation. The interior design mirrors the restaurant’s culinary ethos. With a custom-made open fire grill as the centrepiece, the space exudes a warm, earthy elegance. The open kitchen allows guests to witness the theatricality of fire-based cooking firsthand, enhancing the dining experience. The ambiance is further enriched by the building’s historical charm, blending heritage with contemporary design. Complementing the food is an extensive beverage list featuring a curated selection of local and international wines, as well as craft beers and spirits. The knowledgeable staff are adept at guiding diners through pairings that enhance the flavours of each dish. Firewood is more than a restaurant; it’s an immersive experience that captures the essence of Malaysian cuisine through the elemental force of fire. Under the visionary leadership of Chefs Ker and Lew, it stands as a beacon of culinary excellence in Penang’s vibrant dining scene.

restaurants.name: PRIME + PROPER
restaurants.notes: Detroit’s Modern Cathedral of Steak and Precision In the heart of downtown Detroit, Prime + Proper continues in 2025 to define the new American steakhouse—bold, refined, design-forward and deeply committed to craft. Since opening its doors, it has stood as both a symbol of the city’s culinary renaissance and a national contender for steak restaurant excellence. Housed in a beautifully restored 20th-century building, Prime + Proper exudes modern grandeur—soaring ceilings, marble floors, glass-walled dry-age rooms and a custom-designed butcher counter anchor a dining room where every detail feels curated yet energetic. It’s not just a place to eat—it’s an experience. At the core of that experience is a serious, chef-driven meat programme. All beef is butchered in-house, with an obsessive focus on sourcing, marbling and ageing. Cuts range from grass-fed New York strips and USDA Prime porterhouses to domestic Wagyu and 100+ day dry-aged ribeyes, all grilled over an open flame to precise, smoky perfection. The result is steak with structure, depth and story—and it’s matched by a supporting menu that brings clarity and creativity without overshadowing the fire. Highlights include bone marrow with sourdough ash, butter-poached lobster tail and smoked foie gras torchon, each dish treated with technical care and luxury without excess. The cocktail programme is slick and inventive and the wine list is one of the strongest in the Midwest—featuring deep verticals of Napa, Bordeaux and Italian Barolo, alongside boutique selections and rare finds, all guided by an expert sommelier team. Service is executed with quiet confidence. Staff are polished but never robotic—knowledgeable, stylish and deeply tuned into the tempo of the room, whether it’s a power lunch, romantic dinner, or celebratory feast. Prime + Proper isn’t just one of the best restaurants in Detroit, it’s one of the most exacting steak restaurants in America, where legacy butchery meets modern hospitality and where every steak tells a story of patience, fire and finesse. For those who appreciate luxury with purpose, Prime + Proper is a must.

restaurants.name: THE DEVONSHIRE
restaurants.notes: The Devonshire Soho - a wood-fired steakhouse rooted in British tradition Tucked away on Denman Street in the heart of Soho, The Devonshire has swiftly become one of London’s most sought-after dining destinations. This three-storey establishment masterfully combines the charm of a classic British pub with the sophistication of a modern grill room. The culinary direction of The Devonshire is spearheaded by Ashley Palmer-Watts, renowned for his tenure at The Fat Duck and Dinner by Heston Blumenthal. While Palmer-Watts has meticulously crafted the menu, the daily kitchen operations are entrusted to Head Chef Jamie Guy, formerly of Hix Restaurants. Together, they deliver a menu that celebrates British culinary heritage with contemporary flair. At the core of The Devonshire’s culinary offerings is a bespoke wood ember grill, a rarity in London’s dining scene. This impressive three-metre-long grill uses wood embers instead of charcoal, imparting a distinctive smoky flavour to the dishes. The use of wood embers requires meticulous attention, ensuring each cut of meat is cooked to perfection. The Devonshire takes pride in its in-house butchery, led by Head Butcher George Donnelly. The restaurant sources exceptional Scottish beef, which is dry-aged and butchered on-site. Signature offerings include: • Bone-in Ribeye: A substantial cut, dry-aged to enhance its depth of flavour. • Beef Chop: Weighing up to 1.6kg, this cut showcases the restaurant’s commitment to quality and tradition. Each steak is accompanied by classic sides such as duck fat chips and creamed leeks, reinforcing the establishment’s dedication to British culinary traditions. The Devonshire’s interior exudes warmth and elegance, with wood-panelled walls, white tablecloths and period lighting. The upstairs dining rooms offer a serene atmosphere, providing a contrast to the lively pub below. This setting, combined with attentive service, ensures a memorable dining experience.

restaurants.name: SWIFT & SONS
restaurants.notes: Swift & Sons – Classic Chicago Steakhouse Elegance, Reimagined for a New Generation Set in the historic Fulton Market District, Swift & Sons continues in 2025 to stand tall as one of Chicago’s most polished and iconic modern steakhouses. Named in homage to Gustavus Franklin Swift—one of the pioneers of Chicago’s meatpacking legacy—the restaurant masterfully bridges the gap between past and present, honouring the city’s culinary roots while redefining the art of steakhouse dining with confidence and style. From the moment you step through its doors, Swift & Sons delivers atmosphere in abundance. The space is grand and cinematic: arched ceilings, brass finishes, white tablecloths and a show-stopping central bar give it the weight of tradition, while soft lighting and contemporary details ensure it never feels dated. It’s a room built for both celebration and substance. At the heart of the menu lies a serious and consistent steak programme, built around USDA Prime, dry-aged and hand-selected cuts. The bone-in ribeye, New York strip and 48 oz porterhouse for two remain signature favourites—each grilled with precision, rested with care and plated with a sense of purpose. The char is perfect, the seasoning restrained, allowing the quality of the beef to speak boldly. Beyond the beef, the menu shows impressive breadth: from oysters on the half shell and tuna tartare to butter-poached lobster tail and truffle potato purée, every dish is executed with technical finesse and a light modern touch. The wine programme is deep and thoughtfully global, with standout verticals of California Cabernet, Bordeaux and Super Tuscans. Sommeliers are knowledgeable without being imposing—eager to guide, but always letting the guest set the tone. Service is sharp and polished, reflective of a team that understands the rhythms of classic fine dining but delivers it with a warm, contemporary voice. Whether you’re here for a business dinner, anniversary, or simply a world-class steak, you’re made to feel like you belong. Still Swift & Sons remains a benchmark for American steak restaurant dining -not just in Chicago, but nationally. With its seamless blend of architectural grandeur, meat-driven excellence and timeless hospitality, it’s a place where history, flavour and modern elegance all share the same table.

restaurants.name: THE GRAND BAR & GRILL
restaurants.notes: Set along the stately boulevards of central Helsinki, The Grand Bar & Grill has quietly become Finland’s definitive destination for serious steak and elevated brasserie-style dining. In 2025, it continues to distinguish itself through exceptional sourcing, fire-driven cooking and a uniquely Nordic interpretation of the classic grillroom experience. The space lives up to its name - handsome, warmly lit and tastefully appointed with deep leather seating, natural wood and subtle Scandinavian design flourishes that blend classic elegance with modern restraint. It’s the kind of room that feels both cosmopolitan and distinctly Finnish. At its core, The Grand Bar & Grill is built on a world-class beef programme, featuring a curated selection of cuts from mainly Finland and beyond, with an emphasis on long-aged Northern European dairy cattle and grass-fed Nordic breeds. Each cut is grilled over open flame or glowing coals, with a respect for fat, texture and timing that reflects serious craft. Signature dishes include the dry-aged T-bone steak, a beautifully balanced Finnish Prime Rib on the bone and a rotating selection of rare, limited-batch cuts that speak to the kitchen’s close relationships with regional producers. Steaks arrive rested, sliced with ceremony and served alongside house-made condiments and traditional accompaniments like chimichurri or classic béarnaise. But The Grand isn’t just about meat. The menu showcases seasonal Nordic produce, with starters such as fresh and gratinated oysters, escargots served with sourdough bread and classic beef tartare. Service is graceful, understated and deeply informed. The staff exudes quiet professionalism, allowing guests to settle into the experience with confidence and ease. In 2025, The Grand Bar & Grill stands as a beacon of refined carnivorous dining in the Nordics—a place where fire, sourcing and Nordic seasonality meet in perfect harmony. For those in search of a world-class steak restaurant experience with a distinctly Finnish soul, The Grand is not to be missed.

restaurants.name: GALLAGHERS
restaurants.notes: Gallaghers – Midtown’s Legendary Steakhouse Where New York Tradition Still Sizzles In the ever-evolving landscape of New York dining, Gallaghers stands firm—a timeless shrine to beef, Broadway and old-school swagger. Located just steps from Times Square, this iconic Midtown institution, originally opened in 1927, remains in 2025 a vibrant, living piece of New York’s culinary heritage. Walking into Gallaghers is like stepping into another era—white-tiled floors, dark wood panels and the famous glass-enclosed dry-age room still greet you at the entrance, where rows of prime cuts hang like precious artefacts. The dining room buzzes with the energy of business lunches, theatre-goers and generations of loyal regulars who keep returning for the charm, the service and the steak. At the heart of Gallaghers’ staying power is its unwavering commitment to USDA Prime beef, all aged in-house and broiled at extremely high temperatures for that signature charred crust and juicy centre. The porterhouse for two, the New York strip and the bone-in ribeye remain benchmarks of steakhouse execution—bold, beefy and served without pretense. Supporting dishes stick to the script in the best possible way: clams casino, thick-cut bacon, creamed spinach and hand-cut fries all arrive with classic flair and generous portions. There’s no attempt to reinvent the wheel here—just consistency, tradition and deeply satisfying flavour. The cocktail programme leans into the heritage vibe—think perfectly stirred Manhattans and dry martinis—while the wine list balances big Napa reds and tried-and-true Bordeaux with enough variety to suit every cut and occasion. Service is sharp, friendly and fast-paced, with staff who understand the rhythm of the room and treat every guest like a regular. It’s the kind of hospitality that feels earned over decades, not scripted. Gallaghers continues to prove that true institutions don’t need reinvention—they just need to stay great. For those who love steak served with history, swagger and authenticity, this legendary New York steakhouse still delivers, bite after bite.

restaurants.name: MAVEN
restaurants.notes: Tucked away in a former warehouse complex in the city’s hip Zuid district, Maven has quickly earned its place as Antwerp’s most compelling destination for fire-led, meat-driven dining. In 2025, it continues to set the tone for a new generation of steak restaurants—cool, confident and uncompromising in quality. The space is striking: contemporary but raw, with concrete, steel and exposed textures softened by warm lighting and elegant details. The open kitchen, centred around a massive wood-fired grill, sets the atmosphere ablaze—both literally and creatively. This isn’t your grandfather’s steakhouse—this is Belgian culinary craft with a primal edge. At the heart of Maven’s identity is its unapologetically serious meat programme. Cuts are selected from rare European breeds and sourced with expertise, with a particular focus on mature dairy cows—Rubia Gallega, Simmental and Portuguese Mirandesa —dry-aged in-house for maximum depth and complexity. The results are convincing: a bold, earthy, mineral-rich flavour profile that celebrates fat, fire and tradition. Signature dishes include the bone-in ribeye from Galician beef, the A5 Kobe beef Japanese Wagyu filet and a seasonal rotating selection of lesser-known cuts that reward the curious diner. Steaks are grilled over fire and served with clarity—no distractions, just salt, smoke and time. The wine list is daring and curated with precision—a mix of natural, biodynamic and classic old-world reds, chosen to highlight the richness and structure of the grill. The sommelier team is passionate, intuitive and refreshingly unpretentious. Service at Maven walks the fine line between cool and deeply caring. The staff is stylish, informed and sincere, guiding diners through the experience without affectation. Maven in Antwerp stands as a benchmark for new-era steak restaurants for those who appreciate theatre and bold flavour, true sourcing and a dining room that feels both edgy and elevated, Maven is essential.

restaurants.name: AMERICAN CUT
restaurants.notes: In a city where the steakhouse is practically a civic institution, American Cut once roared into the scene with a swagger all its own—bold, modern and unapologetically theatrical. Nestled in Tribeca, the restaurant quickly became known for its dramatic cuts, glammed-up service and new-school energy. But in 2025, while American Cut still offers much to admire, it finds itself at a crossroads—struggling to fully recapture the momentum and edge that once set it apart. The setting remains striking: a moody, velvet-lined dining room where leather banquettes, dim lighting and polished brass details create an ambiance that’s part supper club, part steakhouse cool.
